following code sections were retrieved: Path: project-requirements.md 1 ## 1. 概述 2 3 基于 Next.js + daisyUI 的智能工作记录与总结小工具，支持中英文国际化，集成 OpenAI 等 LLM 服务，为用户提供高效的工作记录、自动总结及智能助手功能。 4 5 --- 6 7 ## 2. 技术栈 8 9 - **前端**：Next.js (app/目录)、React + daisyUI 10 - **样式**：Tailwind CSS + daisyUI 组件库（所有 size 相关属性统一使用 sm） 11 - **状态管理**：React Query (TanStack Query) - 用于数据获取、缓存和状态管理 12 - **国际化**：next-i18next 或 next-intl 13 - **后端**：Next.js API Routes（分层架构设计，AI 友好） 14 - **数据库**：PostgreSQL (Prisma ORM) 15 - **AI 服务**：Vercel AI SDK + OpenAI API / 其他 LLM 16 - **可视化**：Recharts / Chart.js 17 18 --- 19 20 ## 3. 功能架构设计 21 22 ```mermaid 23 flowchart LR 24 subgraph Frontend["前端架构 (紧凑布局)"] 25 UI[UI Components
daisyUI sm size] 26 RQ[React Query
状态管理 & 缓存] 27 API_CLIENT[API Client
统一接口层] 28 end 29 subgraph Backend["后端分层架构 (AI友好)"] 30 CONTROLLER[Controller Layer
路由处理] 31 SERVICE[Service Layer
业务逻辑] 32 REPOSITORY[Repository Layer
数据访问] 33 DB[(PostgreSQL)] 34 AI[OpenAI API] 35 end 36 UI --> RQ 37 RQ --> API_CLIENT 38 API_CLIENT --> CONTROLLER 39 CONTROLLER --> SERVICE 40 SERVICE --> REPOSITORY 41 SERVICE --> AI 42 REPOSITORY --> DB 43 ``` 44 45 ### 前端架构特点 46 47 - **紧凑布局设计**：所有组件间距、padding、margin 采用紧凑设计 48 - **React Query 状态管理**：统一数据获取、缓存、同步和错误处理 49 - **AI 友好接口层**：标准化的 API 调用接口，便于 AI 理解和维护 50 51 ### 后端分层架构 52 53 - **Controller Layer**：处理 HTTP 请求，参数验证，响应格式化 54 - **Service Layer**：核心业务逻辑，AI 集成，数据处理 55 - **Repository Layer**：数据库操作抽象，查询优化 56 57 ### 核心功能模块 58 59 - **工作记录模块**：CRUD 任务记录；状态更新；标签 & 项目关联 60 - **智能总结模块**：手动触发，支持定期触发；调用 AI 生成报告；展示图表 61 - **Agent 助手模块**：实时分析；预测 & 建议；优先级排序 62 63 --- 64 65 ## 4. 数据库模型设计 66 67 ```prisma 68 model User { 69 id String @id @default(cuid()) 70 name String 71 email String @unique 72 tasks Task[] 73 } 74 75 model Project { 76 id String @id @default(cuid()) 77 name String 78 tasks Task[] 79 } 80 81 model Tag { 82 id String @id @default(cuid()) 83 name String @unique 84 tasks Task[] @relation(references: [id]) 85 } ... 103 ``` 104 105 --- 106 107 ## 5. 主要页面与组件结构 108 109 ```text 110 /src/app # Next.js App Router 111 |_ layout.tsx # 根布局 (紧凑设计) 112 |_ page.tsx # 仪表盘 113 |_ tasks 114 |_ page.tsx # 任务列表 115 |_ [id] 116 |_ page.tsx # 任务详情与编辑 117 |_ summary 118 |_ daily 119 |_ page.tsx # 日报 120 |_ weekly 121 |_ page.tsx # 周报 122 |_ monthly 123 |_ page.tsx # 月报 124 |_ settings 125 |_ page.tsx # 个人设置 / 国际化切换 ... 205 ``` 206 207 ### Repository Layer - 数据访问 208 209 ```typescript 210 // src/repositories/taskRepository.ts 211 export class TaskRepository { 212 constructor(private prisma: PrismaClient) {} 213 214 async findMany(filters: TaskFilters): Promise 215 async findById(id: string): Promise 216 async create(data: CreateTaskData): Promise 217 async update(id: string, data: UpdateTaskData): Promise 218 async delete(id: string): Promise 219 } 220 ``` 221 222 --- 223 224 ## 7. 实现步骤与文件结构 225 226 1. **初始化项目** 227 228 - `npx create-next-app@latest --typescript --app` 229 - 安装依赖：`daisyui`, `tailwindcss`, `prisma`, `@prisma/client`, `@tanstack/react-query`, `ai`, `@ai-sdk/openai`, `next-i18next` 等 230 231 2. **配置 Tailwind & daisyUI (紧凑布局)** 232 233 - 更新 `tailwind.config.js` 234 - 配置 daisyUI 主题，设置默认 size 为 sm 235 - 自定义紧凑布局的 spacing 配置 236 237 3. **React Query 状态管理配置** 238 239 - 配置 QueryClient 和 QueryClientProvider 240 - 设置缓存策略和错误处理 241 - 创建自定义 hooks 封装 API 调用 242 243 4. **国际化集成** 244 245 - 配置 `next-i18next.config.js` 246 - 创建 `public/locales/{en,zh}/common.json` 247 248 5. **数据库 & ORM** 249 250 - 编写 `schema.prisma` 251 - 运行 `npx prisma migrate dev` 252 253 6. **后端分层架构搭建** 254 255 - 创建 Repository Layer (数据访问层) 256 - 创建 Service Layer (业务逻辑层) 257 - 创建 Controller Layer (API 路由层) 258 - 实现依赖注入和错误处理 259 260 7. **前端组件开发 (紧凑设计)** 261 262 - 搭建基本布局与导航 (使用 daisyUI sm size) 263 - 实现任务列表与 CRUD (紧凑布局) 264 - 集成 React Query 进行数据管理 265 266 8. **AI 接口集成 (使用 Vercel AI SDK)** 267 268 - 在 Service Layer 中集成 Vercel AI SDK 269 - 实现流式响应和文本补全功能 270 - 配置 AI 路由使用 Vercel AI SDK 的标准模式 271 - 实现 prompt 模板 & 并发调用控制 272 - 通过 Repository 层访问历史数据 273 274 9. **可视化与报告** 275 276 - 使用 Recharts 绘制时间趋势图、饼图等 277 - 实现紧凑的图表布局 ... 292 293 ``` 294 ├── public 295 │ └── locales 296 │ ├── en 297 │ └── zh 298 ├── prisma 299 │ └── schema.prisma 300 ├── src 301 │ ├── app # Next.js App Router 302 │ │ ├── layout.tsx # 根布局 (紧凑设计) 303 │ │ ├── page.tsx # 仪表盘 304 │ │ ├── tasks/ 305 │ │ │ ├── page.tsx 306 │ │ │ └── [id]/page.tsx 307 │ │ ├── summary/ 308 │ │ │ ├── daily/page.tsx 309 │ │ │ ├── weekly/page.tsx 310 │ │ │ └── monthly/page.tsx 311 │ │ ├── settings/ 312 │ │ │ └── page.tsx 313 │ │ └── api # API Routes (App Router) 314 │ │ ├── tasks/ 315 │ │ │ ├── route.ts 316 │ │ │ └── [id]/route.ts 317 │ │ ├── summary/ 318 │ │ │ └── route.ts 319 │ │ ├── agent/ 320 │ │ │ └── suggestions/route.ts ... 337 │ ├── repositories # Repository Layer (数据访问) 338 │ │ ├── taskRepository.ts 339 │ │ ├── userRepository.ts 340 │ │ └── base/ 341 │ ├── types # TypeScript类型定义 342 │ │ ├── api.ts 343 │ │ ├── task.ts 344 │ │ ├── summary.ts 345 │ │ └── ai.ts # AI相关类型 346 │ ├── styles # 样式文件 (紧凑主题) 347 │ └── utils # 工具函数 348 ├── tailwind.config.js # Tailwind配置 (紧凑spacing) 349 ├── next-i18next.config.js 350 ├── package.json 351 └── README.md 352 ``` 353 354 ## 8. AI 集成与第三方框架文档获取 355 356 ### Vercel AI SDK 集成规范 357 358 所有 AI 相关的路由都必须使用 Vercel AI SDK，包括： 359 360 ```typescript 361 // src/app/api/ai/chat/route.ts (使用 Vercel AI SDK) 362 import { openai } from '@ai-sdk/openai' 363 import { streamText } from 'ai' ... 427 ``` 428 429 --- 430 431 ## 9. 设计原则总结 432 433 ### UI/UX 设计原则 434 435 - **紧凑布局**：所有组件使用 daisyUI 的 sm size，减少不必要的空白 436 - **一致性**：统一的间距、字体大小和组件尺寸 437 - **响应式**：在紧凑的基础上保持良好的移动端体验 438 439 ### 架构设计原则 440 441 - **分层清晰**：Controller → Service → Repository 三层架构 442 - **AI 友好**：标准化的接口、清晰的命名、完整的类型定义 443 - **可维护性**：单一职责、依赖注入、错误处理统一 444 - **性能优化**：React Query 缓存、数据库查询优化 445 446 ### 开发规范 447 448 - **TypeScript 严格模式**：确保类型安全 449 - **统一的错误处理**：标准化的错误响应格式 450 - **API 文档化**：清晰的接口定义和注释 451 - **测试覆盖**：单元测试和集成测试 452 - **第三方框架使用**：开发时需参考最新的官方文档，确保使用最佳实践 453 - Vercel AI SDK: 流式响应和文本生成的标准实现 454 - React Query: 数据获取、缓存和状态同步 455 - daisyUI: 组件 size 属性统一使用 sm 456 - Prisma: 数据库操作和类型安全 457 458 --- 459 460 以上优化方案在原有功能基础上，强化了紧凑布局设计、React Query 状态管理、后端分层架构等关键特性，使项目更加 AI 友好且具备良好的扩展性与可维护性。 ... Path: README.md ... 18 19 1. **Dashboard**: Overview of task statistics, recent tasks, and quick actions 20 2. **Task Management**: Create, edit, delete, and track tasks with various filters 21 3. **AI Assistant**: Generate task suggestions and work summaries 22 4. **Summary Reports**: Daily, weekly, and monthly productivity reports 23 5. **Project Management**: Organize tasks into projects for better workflow 24 25 ## Tech Stack 26 27 - **Frontend**: Next.js 15, React 19, TypeScript 28 - **Styling**: Tailwind CSS 4, DaisyUI (compact layout) 29 - **State Management**: React Query (TanStack Query) 30 - **Database**: SQLite with Prisma ORM 31 - **AI Integration**: Vercel AI SDK with OpenAI 32 - **Internationalization**: next-intl 33 - **Package Manager**: pnpm ... Path: prisma/schema.prisma 1 // This is your Prisma schema file, 2 // learn more about it in the docs: https://pris.ly/d/prisma-schema 3 4 generator client { 5 provider = "prisma-client-js" 6 } 7 8 datasource db { 9 provider = "sqlite" 10 url = env("DATABASE_URL") 11 } 12 13 model User { 14 id String @id @default(cuid()) 15 email String @unique 16 name String? 17 avatar String? 18 createdAt DateTime @default(now()) 19 updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt 20 21 // 关联关系 22 projects Project[] 23 tasks Task[] 24 tags Tag[] 25 26 @@map("users") 27 } 28 29 model Project { 30 id String @id @default(cuid()) 31 name String 32 description String? 33 color String? // 项目颜色标识 34 isActive Boolean @default(true) 35 createdAt DateTime @default(now()) 36 updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt 37 38 // 外键 39 userId String 40 user User @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade) 41 42 // 关联关系 43 tasks Task[] 44 45 @@map("projects") 46 } 47 48 model Tag { 49 id String @id @default(cuid()) 50 name String 51 color String? // 标签颜色 52 createdAt DateTime @default(now()) 53 updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt 54 55 // 外键 56 userId String 57 user User @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade) 58 59 // 关联关系 60 tasks TaskTag[] 61 62 @@unique([userId, name]) // 同一用户下标签名唯一 63 @@map("tags") 64 } 65 66 model Task { 67 id String @id @default(cuid()) 68 title String 69 description String? 70 status TaskStatus @default(PENDING) 71 priority Priority @default(MEDIUM) 72 startTime DateTime? 73 endTime DateTime? 74 duration Int? // 持续时间（分钟） 75 createdAt DateTime @default(now()) 76 updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt 77 78 // 外键 79 userId String 80 user User @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade) 81 projectId String? 82 project Project? @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull) 83 84 // 关联关系 85 tags TaskTag[] 86 87 @@map("tasks") 88 } 89 90 model TaskTag { 91 id String @id @default(cuid()) 92 taskId String 93 tagId String 94 95 task Task @relation(fields: [taskId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade) 96 tag Tag @relation(fields: [tagId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade) 97 98 @@unique([taskId, tagId]) 99 @@map("task_tags") 100 } 101 102 enum TaskStatus { 103 PENDING 104 IN_PROGRESS 105 COMPLETED 106 CANCELED 107 } 108 109 enum Priority { 110 LOW 111 MEDIUM 112 HIGH 113 } ... Path: src/lib/repositories/tag.repository.ts ... 20 21 export class TagRepository extends BaseRepository { 22 constructor() { 23 super('tag') 24 } 25 26 async create(data: CreateTagInput): Promise { 27 const tag = await this.prisma.tag.create({ 28 data, 29 include: { 30 _count: { 31 select: { 32 tasks: true 33 } 34 } 35 } 36 }) 37 38 return tag 39 } 40 41 async findById(id: string): Promise { 42 const tag = await this.prisma.tag.findUnique({ 43 where: { id }, 44 include: { 45 _count: { 46 select: { 47 tasks: true 48 } 49 } 50 } 51 }) 52 53 return tag 54 } 55 56 async findMany(options: { 57 where?: any 58 orderBy?: any 59 skip?: number 60 take?: number 61 } = {}): Promise { 62 const tags = await this.prisma.tag.findMany({ 63 ...options, 64 include: { 65 _count: { 66 select: { 67 tasks: true 68 } 69 } 70 } 71 }) 72 73 return tags 74 } ... Path: src/lib/services/project.service.ts 1 import { BaseService } from './base.service' 2 import { 3 ProjectRepository, 4 ProjectWithStats, 5 CreateProjectInput, 6 UpdateProjectInput 7 } from '../repositories/project.repository' 8 9 export class ProjectService extends BaseService { 10 private projectRepository: ProjectRepository 11 12 constructor() { 13 super() 14 this.projectRepository = new ProjectRepository() 15 } 16 17 async createProject(data: CreateProjectInput): Promise { 18 try { 19 this.validateRequiredFields(data, ['name', 'userId']) 20 21 const sanitizedData = this.sanitizeInput(data) as CreateProjectInput 22 23 return await this.projectRepository.create(sanitizedData) 24 } catch (error) { 25 this.handleError(error, 'ProjectService.createProject') 26 } 27 } ... **技术栈**：daisyUI、lucide-react、React Query、next-intl - **状态管理**：React Query (TanStack Query) - **样式**：Tailwind CSS + daisyUI (紧凑布局，统一使用sm size) ### 数据库 - **SQLite + Prisma ORM** - Complex React/Next.js Apps Loading... [Posts](/) [Topics](/topics) [Newsletter](https://trevorlasn.substack.com/) Search Close Up to date Published May 4, 2025 12 min read ![Author Trevor I. Lasn](https://res.cloudinary.com/da7jhtpgh/image/upload/c_fill,w_48,h_48,q_auto:best,f_auto,dpr_2.0/v1725532129/11220736_pdgixq.webp "Click to enlarge") Trevor I. Lasn Staff Software Engineer, Engineering Manager Robust Data Fetching Architecture For Complex React/Next.js Apps ================================================================ How I use the 'Three Layers of Data' architecture pattern for React and Next.js apps to avoid common pitfalls, tech debt, and improve performance ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Most of us underestimate data fetching complexity until it’s too late. Many projects begin innocently with `useEffect()` and `fetch()` sprinkled across components. Before you know it, the growing tangle of error handlers, loading states, memoization, and caching logic turns your code into a debugging nightmare. Here are common issues I see in many apps: **\[1\]** Components firing duplicate network requests because someone forgot to cache the result **\[2\]** Components re-rendering dozens of times per second from poorly managed state **\[3\]** Too many skeleton loaders, making the app feel perpetually slow **\[4\]** Users seeing stale data for seconds after mutations because cache invalidation is broken **\[5\]** Race conditions when parallel queries return in unexpected order **\[6\]** Memory leaks from uncleared subscriptions and event listeners that never get cleaned up **\[7\]** Optimistic updates that fail silently, leaving data inconsistent **\[8\]** Server-side data that goes stale immediately on navigation **\[9\]** Polling for updates that never happen, or worse, poll every second when the data is static **\[10\]** Components that are too tightly coupled to their data fetching logic, making them hard to reuse **\[11\]** Sequential network requests where each query depends on the previous result, e.g get user → get user’s org → get org’s teams → get team members …and so on. These issues compound. A single badly architected data fetching pattern spawns three more problems. Before you know it, a “simple” dashboard needs refactoring from the ground up. This article shows you a better way, or atleast how I prefer to structure my apps. We’ll build three layers of data fetching that scale from simple CRUD to complex, real-time applications without breaking your mental model. But before we visit the three layer data approach; Your first instinct might be to toss `useEffect()` and `fetch()` into a component and move on. Here’s why this approach derails fast: The **many** problems with this approach: * Waterfall: Requests execute sequentially (more on this later) * State management chaos: Multiple `useState` hooks that can get out of sync * Memory leaks: Event listeners, intervals need manual cleanup * No request cancellation: How do you cancel in-flight requests on unmount? * Complex loading states: When is isLoading true/false? Which loading? * Error boundaries: Where do errors bubble up to? * Cache staleness: No way to mark data as stale * Optimistic update nightmare: Manual rollback logic needed * Dependency arrays: Easy to miss dependencies and cause bugs * Testing complexity: Mocking all these effects is a nightmare This mess grows exponentially as your app scales. Every new feature adds more state, more effects, and more edge cases to handle. Sure, you can use libraries like Redux or MobX to manage state, but they add complexity and boilerplate. You end up with a tangled web of actions, reducers, and selectors that are hard to follow. (I like both libraries, but they are not the solution to this problem) You might be thinking, “But I can just use `useReducer()` and `useContext()` to manage this!” Sure, but that doesn’t solve the underlying problem of data fetching complexity. You still have to deal with the same issues of loading states, error handling, and cache invalidation. On a another note, you might be thinking why cant’t I just fetch all the data in one go? Why not just do this? Server components are faster and more efficient way of doing this. They allow you to fetch data on the server and send it to the client in one go, reducing the number of network requests and improving performance. What if I told you there was a better way? A way to structure your data fetching that scales with your app and keeps your components clean and focused. This is where the **Three Layers of Data Architecture** come in. This pattern separates your data fetching into three distinct layers, each with its own responsibilities. This makes your app easier to reason about, test, and maintain. ### Three Layers of Data Architecture The solution is to build a three-layer architecture that separates concerns and makes your app easier to reason about. This architecture is inspired by the principles of [React Query](https://tanstack.com/query/latest/docs/framework/react/overview), which provides a powerful way to manage server state in your application. You don’t have to use React Query, but it’s my go-to library for data fetching and caching. It handles a lot of the boilerplate for you, so you can focus on building your app. **Note:** If you decide to use React Query, don’t forget to use [``](https://tanstack.com/query/latest/docs/framework/react/devtools) in development. It makes debugging a breeze. ![React Query Devtools](https://res.cloudinary.com/da7jhtpgh/image/upload/t_googleimg/v1746379578/Screenshot_2025-05-04_at_20.24.48_ehsmb6.webp) Back to the three layers. The architecture is simple: 1. **Server Components** - Initial data fetching 2. **React Query** - Client-side caching and updates 3. **Optimistic Updates** - Instant UI feedback > React Query provides two ways to optimistically update your UI before a mutation has completed. You can either use the `onMutate` option to update your cache directly, or leverage the returned variables to update your UI from the `useMutation` result. Here’s a folder structure to help illustrate the three layers: ### How Data Flows: The 3-Layer Architecture The three layers work in sequence but remain independent: ### Layer 1: Server Components Server Components handle initial data fetching, making your app feel instant. But they don’t update dynamically - that’s where React Query enters (Layer 2). The `getAllTeams` function is a simple database query that fetches all teams. It can be a simple SQL query or an ORM call, depending on your setup. ### Layer 2: React Query Layer 2 consumes the initial data from Layer 1 and manages client-side state: And here’s how a client-side component would consume from layer 2. ### Layer 3: Optimistic Updates Layer 3 is where the magic happens. It allows you to update the UI instantly while the server processes the request. This is done using optimistic updates. Mutations are handled in a separate hook, `useTeamMutations`, which uses React Query’s `useMutation` to handle the creation and deletion of teams. The `TeamCard` component uses the `useTeamMutations` hook to handle team creation and deletion. It also shows loading states for each action. ### Context: Tying It All Together Context providers eliminate prop drilling and centralize data access. This is especially useful for complex apps with multiple components needing the same data. The `OrganizationProvider` wraps the `TeamList` component, providing it with the initial data from Layer 1 and managing the loading state and errors. For a more complex app, you can add more context providers for different data layers. For example, you might have a `UserContext` for user data and an `AuthContext` for authentication state. All this allows you to keep your components clean and focused on rendering, while the data fetching and state management is handled in a centralized way. The three layer data approach is over kill for simple apps, but it scales well for larger applications. It also makes testing easier, as you can mock the context providers and test components in isolation. _P.S:_ You can use a similar approach with Vue.js, Svelte, or any other framework. The key is to separate concerns and keep your components focused on rendering. * * * [ Found this article helpful? You might enjoy my free newsletter. Layer Code Generation Rules\n\n## \u6982\u8ff0\nRepository\u5c42\u662f\u6570\u636e\u8bbf\u95ee\u5c42\uff0c\u8d1f\u8d23\u4e0e\u6570\u636e\u5e93\u4ea4\u4e92\uff0c\u63d0\u4f9b\u7edf\u4e00\u7684\u6570\u636e\u8bbf\u95ee\u63a5\u53e3\u3002\u57fa\u4e8ePrisma ORM\u5b9e\u73b0\uff0c\u7ee7\u627fBaseRepository\u57fa\u7c7b\u3002\n\n## \u6280\u672f\u6808\n- **ORM**: Prisma Client\n- **\u6570\u636e\u5e93**: SQLite\n- **\u57fa\u7c7b**: BaseRepository\n- **\u7c7b\u578b\u5b89\u5168**: TypeScript\u4e25\u683c\u6a21\u5f0f\n\n## \u6587\u4ef6\u7ed3\u6784\n```\nsrc/lib/repositories/\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 base.repository.ts # \u57fa\u7840Repository\u7c7b\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 user.repository.ts # \u7528\u6237\u6570\u636e\u8bbf\u95ee\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 project.repository.ts # \u9879\u76ee\u6570\u636e\u8bbf\u95ee\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 task.repository.ts # \u4efb\u52a1\u6570\u636e\u8bbf\u95ee\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 tag.repository.ts # \u6807\u7b7e\u6570\u636e\u8bbf\u95ee\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 [entity].repository.ts # \u65b0\u5b9e\u4f53\u6570\u636e\u8bbf\u95ee\n```\n\n## \u4ee3\u7801\u751f\u6210\u89c4\u5219\n\n### 1. \u57fa\u7840\u7ed3\u6784\u6a21\u677f\n\n```typescript\nimport { BaseRepository } from './base.repository'\nimport { prisma } from '../prisma'\nimport type { [Entity], [EntityStatus], [EntityPriority] } from '@prisma/client'\n\n// \u7c7b\u578b\u5b9a\u4e49\nexport type [Entity]WithRelations = [Entity] & {\n // \u5173\u8054\u5173\u7cfb\u7c7b\u578b\u5b9a\u4e49\n [relation]?: [RelationType]\n [relationArray]?: [RelationType][]\n}\n\nexport type Create[Entity]Input = {\n // \u521b\u5efa\u8f93\u5165\u7c7b\u578b\n [field]: [type]\n [optionalField]?: [type]\n}\n\nexport type Update[Entity]Input = {\n // \u66f4\u65b0\u8f93\u5165\u7c7b\u578b\n [field]?: [type]\n}\n\nexport type [Entity]Filters = {\n // \u8fc7\u6ee4\u6761\u4ef6\u7c7b\u578b\n userId: string\n [filterField]?: [type]\n startDate?: Date\n endDate?: Date\n search?: string\n}\n\nexport class [Entity]Repository extends BaseRepository<[Entity]WithRelations, Create[Entity]Input, Update[Entity]Input> {\n constructor() {\n super('[entity]')\n }\n\n // \u5b9e\u73b0\u5177\u4f53\u65b9\u6cd5...\n}\n```\n\n### 2. \u5fc5\u9700\u65b9\u6cd5\u5b9e\u73b0\n\n#### \u521b\u5efa\u65b9\u6cd5\n```typescript\nasync create(data: Create[Entity]Input): Promise<[Entity]WithRelations> {\n const { [relationIds], ...[entity]Data } = data\n \n const [entity] = await this.prisma.[entity].create({\n data: {\n ...[entity]Data,\n [relations]: [relationIds] ? {\n create: [relationIds].map([relationId] => ({\n [relationId]\n }))\n } : undefined\n },\n include: {\n [relation]: {\n select: {\n id: true,\n name: true,\n [additionalFields]: true\n }\n },\n [relationArray]: {\n include: {\n [nestedRelation]: {\n select: {\n id: true,\n name: true,\n [additionalFields]: true\n }\n }\n }\n }\n }\n })\n\n return this.transform[Entity]([entity])\n}\n```\n\n#### \u67e5\u8be2\u65b9\u6cd5\n```typescript\nasync findById(id: string): Promise<[Entity]WithRelations | null> {\n const [entity] = await this.prisma.[entity].findUnique({\n where: { id },\n include: {\n [relation]: {\n select: {\n id: true,\n name: true,\n [additionalFields]: true\n }\n },\n [relationArray]: {\n include: {\n [nestedRelation]: {\n select: {\n id: true,\n name: true,\n [additionalFields]: true\n }\n }\n }\n }\n }\n })\n\n if (![entity]) return null\n\n return this.transform[Entity]([entity])\n}\n\nasync findMany(options: {\n where?: any\n orderBy?: any\n skip?: number\n take?: number\n} = {}): Promise<[Entity]WithRelations[]> {\n const [entities] = await this.prisma.[entity].findMany({\n ...options,\n include: {\n [relation]: {\n select: {\n id: true,\n name: true,\n [additionalFields]: true\n }\n },\n [relationArray]: {\n include: {\n [nestedRelation]: {\n select: {\n id: true,\n name: true,\n [additionalFields]: true\n }\n }\n }\n }\n }\n })\n\n return [entities].map([entity] => this.transform[Entity]([entity]))\n}\n```\n\n#### \u8fc7\u6ee4\u67e5\u8be2\u65b9\u6cd5\n```typescript\nasync findByFilters(filters: [Entity]Filters): Promise<[Entity]WithRelations[]> {\n const { userId, [filterField], startDate, endDate, search, [relationIds] } = filters\n \n const where: any = {\n userId,\n ...[filterField] && { [filterField] },\n ...startDate && endDate && {\n createdAt: {\n gte: startDate,\n lte: endDate\n }\n },\n ...search && {\n OR: [\n { title: { contains: search, mode: 'insensitive' } },\n { description: { contains: search, mode: 'insensitive' } }\n ]\n },\n ...[relationIds] && [relationIds].length > 0 && {\n [relations]: {\n some: {\n [relationId]: {\n in: [relationIds]\n }\n }\n }\n }\n }\n\n return this.findMany({\n where,\n orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' }\n })\n}\n```\n\n### 3. \u6570\u636e\u8f6c\u6362\u65b9\u6cd5\n\n```typescript\nprivate transform[Entity](raw[Entity]: any): [Entity]WithRelations {\n return {\n ...raw[Entity],\n [relationArray]: raw[Entity].[relationArray]?.map((item: any) => ({\n id: item.[nestedRelation].id,\n name: item.[nestedRelation].name,\n [additionalField]: item.[nestedRelation].[additionalField]\n })) || []\n }\n}\n```\n\n### 4. \u7edf\u8ba1\u65b9\u6cd5\n\n```typescript\nasync getStats(userId: string): Promise<[Entity]Stats> {\n const [totalCount, statusCounts] = await Promise.all([\n this.prisma.[entity].count({\n where: { userId }\n }),\n this.prisma.[entity].groupBy({\n by: ['status'],\n where: { userId },\n _count: true\n })\n ])\n\n return {\n total: totalCount,\n byStatus: statusCounts.reduce((acc, item) => {\n acc[item.status] = item._count\n return acc\n }, {} as Record<[EntityStatus], number>)\n }\n}\n```\n\n## \u547d\u540d\u89c4\u8303\n\n### \u6587\u4ef6\u547d\u540d\n- \u6587\u4ef6\u540d\uff1a`[entity].repository.ts` (\u5c0f\u5199\uff0c\u5355\u6570)\n- \u7c7b\u540d\uff1a`[Entity]Repository` (PascalCase\uff0c\u5355\u6570)\n\n### \u7c7b\u578b\u547d\u540d\n- \u4e3b\u7c7b\u578b\uff1a`[Entity]WithRelations`\n- \u8f93\u5165\u7c7b\u578b\uff1a`Create[Entity]Input`, `Update[Entity]Input`\n- \u8fc7\u6ee4\u7c7b\u578b\uff1a`[Entity]Filters`\n- \u7edf\u8ba1\u7c7b\u578b\uff1a`[Entity]Stats`\n\n### \u65b9\u6cd5\u547d\u540d\n- \u521b\u5efa\uff1a`create`\n- \u67e5\u8be2\uff1a`findById`, `findMany`, `findByFilters`\n- \u66f4\u65b0\uff1a`update`\n- \u5220\u9664\uff1a`delete`\n- \u7edf\u8ba1\uff1a`getStats`, `get[Specific]Stats`\n- \u8f6c\u6362\uff1a`transform[Entity]` (\u79c1\u6709\u65b9\u6cd5)\n\n## \u9519\u8bef\u5904\u7406\n\n### Prisma\u9519\u8bef\u7801\u5904\u7406\n```typescript\n// \u5728BaseRepository\u4e2d\u7edf\u4e00\u5904\u7406\nprotected handlePrismaError(error: any): never {\n if (error.code === 'P2002') {\n throw new Error('Record already exists')\n }\n if (error.code === 'P2025') {\n throw new Error('Record not found')\n }\n if (error.code === 'P2003') {\n throw new Error('Foreign key constraint failed')\n }\n throw error\n}\n```\n\n## \u6027\u80fd\u4f18\u5316\n\n### \u67e5\u8be2\u4f18\u5316\n1. **\u9009\u62e9\u6027\u5305\u542b**\uff1a\u53ea\u5305\u542b\u9700\u8981\u7684\u5173\u8054\u6570\u636e\n2. **\u5206\u9875\u652f\u6301**\uff1a\u4f7f\u7528skip\u548ctake\u53c2\u6570\n3. **\u7d22\u5f15\u5229\u7528**\uff1a\u786e\u4fddwhere\u6761\u4ef6\u4f7f\u7528\u6570\u636e\u5e93\u7d22\u5f15\n4. **\u6279\u91cf\u64cd\u4f5c**\uff1a\u4f7f\u7528createMany, updateMany\u7b49\u6279\u91cf\u65b9\u6cd5\n\n### \u7f13\u5b58\u7b56\u7565\n```typescript\n// \u5728\u9700\u8981\u7684\u5730\u65b9\u6dfb\u52a0\u7f13\u5b58\u903b\u8f91\nprivate cache = new Map()\n\nasync findByIdCached(id: string): Promise<[Entity]WithRelations | null> {\n if (this.cache.has(id)) {\n return this.cache.get(id)!\n }\n \n const result = await this.findById(id)\n if (result) {\n this.cache.set(id, result)\n }\n \n return result\n}\n```\n\n## \u6d4b\u8bd5\u89c4\u8303\n\n### \u5355\u5143\u6d4b\u8bd5\u7ed3\u6784\n```typescript\ndescribe('[Entity]Repository', () => {\n let repository: [Entity]Repository\n \n beforeEach(() => {\n repository = new [Entity]Repository()\n })\n \n describe('create', () => {\n it('should create [entity] with relations', async () => {\n // \u6d4b\u8bd5\u903b\u8f91\n })\n })\n \n describe('findByFilters', () => {\n it('should filter by [filterField]', async () => {\n // \u6d4b\u8bd5\u903b\u8f91\n })\n })\n})\n```\n\n## \u4f7f\u7528\u793a\u4f8b\n\n```typescript\n// \u5728Service\u5c42\u4e2d\u4f7f\u7528\nexport class [Entity]Service {\n private [entity]Repository: [Entity]Repository\n \n constructor() {\n this.[entity]Repository = new [Entity]Repository()\n }\n \n async create[Entity](data: Create[Entity]Input): Promise<[Entity]WithRelations> {\n return await this.[entity]Repository.create(data)\n }\n}\n```"}","mcp_server_name":"","mcp_tool_name":""}},{"id":0,"type":0,"content":""},{"id":2,"type":2,"content":""},{"id":3,"type":3,"content":""}],"seen_state":"seen","structured_request_nodes":[{"id":1,"type":1,"tool_result_node":{"content":"Here's IF MORE CONTENT NEEDS TO BE ADDED USE THE str-replace-editor TOOL TO EDIT THE FILE AFTER IT HAS BEEN CREATED.", "path": "instructions/generate-service-layer-rule.md", "file_content": "# Service Layer Code Generation Rules\n\n## \u6982\u8ff0\nService\u5c42\u662f\u4e1a\u52a1\u903b\u8f91\u5c42\uff0c\u8d1f\u8d23\u5904\u7406\u590d\u6742\u7684\u4e1a\u52a1\u89c4\u5219\u3001\u6570\u636e\u9a8c\u8bc1\u3001\u6743\u9650\u63a7\u5236\u548c\u9519\u8bef\u5904\u7406\u3002\u7ee7\u627fBaseService\u57fa\u7c7b\uff0c\u8c03\u7528Repository\u5c42\u8fdb\u884c\u6570\u636e\u64cd\u4f5c\u3002\n\n## \u6280\u672f\u6808\n- **\u57fa\u7c7b**: BaseService\n- **\u9a8c\u8bc1**: \u5185\u7f6e\u9a8c\u8bc1\u65b9\u6cd5\n- **\u9519\u8bef\u5904\u7406**: \u7edf\u4e00\u9519\u8bef\u5904\u7406\u673a\u5236\n- **\u6743\u9650\u63a7\u5236**: \u7528\u6237\u8bbf\u95ee\u6743\u9650\u9a8c\u8bc1\n- **\u7c7b\u578b\u5b89\u5168**: TypeScript\u4e25\u683c\u6a21\u5f0f\n\n## \u6587\u4ef6\u7ed3\u6784\n```\nsrc/lib/services/\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 base.service.ts # \u57fa\u7840Service\u7c7b\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 user.service.ts # \u7528\u6237\u4e1a\u52a1\u903b\u8f91\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 project.service.ts # \u9879\u76ee\u4e1a\u52a1\u903b\u8f91\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 task.service.ts # \u4efb\u52a1\u4e1a\u52a1\u903b\u8f91\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 tag.service.ts # \u6807\u7b7e\u4e1a\u52a1\u903b\u8f91\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 ai.service.ts # AI\u96c6\u6210\u670d\u52a1\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 [entity].service.ts # \u65b0\u5b9e\u4f53\u4e1a\u52a1\u903b\u8f91\n```\n\n## \u4ee3\u7801\u751f\u6210\u89c4\u5219\n\n### 1. \u57fa\u7840\u7ed3\u6784\u6a21\u677f\n\n```typescript\nimport { BaseService } from './base.service'\nimport { \n [Entity]Repository, \n [Entity]WithRelations, \n Create[Entity]Input, \n Update[Entity]Input,\n [Entity]Filters \n} from '../repositories/[entity].repository'\n\nexport class [Entity]Service extends BaseService {\n private [entity]Repository: [Entity]Repository\n\n constructor() {\n super()\n this.[entity]Repository = new [Entity]Repository()\n }\n\n // \u4e1a\u52a1\u65b9\u6cd5\u5b9e\u73b0...\n}\n```\n\n### 2. CRUD\u4e1a\u52a1\u65b9\u6cd5\n\n#### \u521b\u5efa\u65b9\u6cd5\n```typescript\nasync create[Entity](data: Create[Entity]Input): Promise<[Entity]WithRelations> {\n try {\n // 1. \u9a8c\u8bc1\u5fc5\u9700\u5b57\u6bb5\n this.validateRequiredFields(data, ['[requiredField1]', '[requiredField2]', 'userId'])\n \n // 2. \u6570\u636e\u6e05\u7406\n const sanitizedData = this.sanitizeInput(data) as Create[Entity]Input\n \n // 3. \u4e1a\u52a1\u89c4\u5219\u9a8c\u8bc1\n await this.validate[Entity]BusinessRules(sanitizedData)\n \n // 4. \u521b\u5efa\u5b9e\u4f53\n return await this.[entity]Repository.create(sanitizedData)\n } catch (error) {\n this.handleError(error, '[Entity]Service.create[Entity]')\n }\n}\n```\n\n#### \u67e5\u8be2\u65b9\u6cd5\n```typescript\nasync get[Entity]ById(id: string, userId: string): Promise<[Entity]WithRelations> {\n try {\n this.validateRequiredFields({ id, userId }, ['id', 'userId'])\n \n const [entity] = await this.[entity]Repository.findById(id)\n if (![entity]) {\n throw new Error('[Entity] not found')\n }\n \n // \u9a8c\u8bc1\u7528\u6237\u6743\u9650\n this.validateUserAccess([entity].userId, userId)\n \n return [entity]\n } catch (error) {\n this.handleError(error, '[Entity]Service.get[Entity]ById')\n }\n}\n\nasync get[Entity]List(\n filters: [Entity]Filters, \n page?: number, \n limit?: number\n): Promise<{\n data: [Entity]WithRelations[]\n total: number\n page: number\n limit: number\n totalPages: number\n}> {\n try {\n const { page: validatedPage, limit: validatedLimit } = this.validatePaginationParams(page, limit)\n \n const [entities] = await this.[entity]Repository.findByFilters(filters)\n \n // \u624b\u52a8\u5206\u9875\n const startIndex = (validatedPage - 1) * validatedLimit\n const endIndex = startIndex + validatedLimit\n const paginated[Entities] = [entities].slice(startIndex, endIndex)\n \n return {\n data: paginated[Entities],\n total: [entities].length,\n page: validatedPage,\n limit: validatedLimit,\n totalPages: Math.ceil([entities].length / validatedLimit)\n }\n } catch (error) {\n this.handleError(error, '[Entity]Service.get[Entity]List')\n }\n}\n```\n\n#### \u66f4\u65b0\u65b9\u6cd5\n```typescript\nasync update[Entity](id: string, data: Update[Entity]Input, userId: string): Promise<[Entity]WithRelations> {\n try {\n this.validateRequiredFields({ id, userId }, ['id', 'userId'])\n \n // \u9a8c\u8bc1\u5b9e\u4f53\u5b58\u5728\u548c\u6743\u9650\n const existing[Entity] = await this.get[Entity]ById(id, userId)\n \n // \u6570\u636e\u6e05\u7406\n const sanitizedData = this.sanitizeInput(data) as Update[Entity]Input\n \n // \u4e1a\u52a1\u89c4\u5219\u9a8c\u8bc1\n await this.validate[Entity]UpdateRules(existing[Entity], sanitizedData)\n \n return await this.[entity]Repository.update(id, sanitizedData)\n } catch (error) {\n this.handleError(error, '[Entity]Service.update[Entity]')\n }\n}\n```\n\n#### \u5220\u9664\u65b9\u6cd5\n```typescript\nasync delete[Entity](id: string, userId: string): Promise {\n try {\n this.validateRequiredFields({ id, userId }, ['id', 'userId'])\n \n // \u9a8c\u8bc1\u5b9e\u4f53\u5b58\u5728\u548c\u6743\u9650\n await this.get[Entity]ById(id, userId)\n \n // \u4e1a\u52a1\u89c4\u5219\u9a8c\u8bc1\uff08\u5982\u68c0\u67e5\u662f\u5426\u6709\u5173\u8054\u6570\u636e\uff09\n await this.validate[Entity]DeletionRules(id)\n \n return await this.[entity]Repository.delete(id)\n } catch (error) {\n this.handleError(error, '[Entity]Service.delete[Entity]')\n }\n}\n```\n\n### 3. \u4e1a\u52a1\u89c4\u5219\u9a8c\u8bc1\u65b9\u6cd5\n\n```typescript\nprivate async validate[Entity]BusinessRules(data: Create[Entity]Input): Promise {\n // 1. \u6570\u636e\u683c\u5f0f\u9a8c\u8bc1\n if (data.[field] && data.[field].length > [maxLength]) {\n throw new Error('[Field] is too long')\n }\n \n // 2. \u4e1a\u52a1\u903b\u8f91\u9a8c\u8bc1\n if (data.[relatedField]) {\n const related = await this.[relatedRepository].findById(data.[relatedField])\n if (!related) {\n throw new Error('Related [entity] not found')\n }\n \n // \u9a8c\u8bc1\u5173\u8054\u5b9e\u4f53\u7684\u6743\u9650\n this.validateUserAccess(related.userId, data.userId)\n }\n \n // 3. \u552f\u4e00\u6027\u9a8c\u8bc1\n const existing = await this.[entity]Repository.findMany({\n where: {\n userId: data.userId,\n [uniqueField]: data.[uniqueField]\n }\n })\n \n if (existing.length > 0) {\n throw new Error('[Entity] with this [uniqueField] already exists')\n }\n}\n\nprivate async validate[Entity]UpdateRules(\n existing[Entity]: [Entity]WithRelations, \n updateData: Update[Entity]Input\n): Promise {\n // \u72b6\u6001\u8f6c\u6362\u9a8c\u8bc1\n if (updateData.status && updateData.status !== existing[Entity].status) {\n this.validateStatusTransition(existing[Entity].status, updateData.status)\n }\n \n // \u5176\u4ed6\u66f4\u65b0\u89c4\u5219...\n}\n\nprivate async validate[Entity]DeletionRules(id: string): Promise {\n // \u68c0\u67e5\u662f\u5426\u6709\u5173\u8054\u6570\u636e\n const relatedCount = await this.[relatedRepository].count({\n where: { [entityId]: id }\n })\n \n if (relatedCount > 0) {\n throw new Error('Cannot delete [entity] with related [relatedEntities]')\n }\n}\n```\n\n### 4. \u72b6\u6001\u7ba1\u7406\u65b9\u6cd5\n\n```typescript\nprivate validateStatusTransition(currentStatus: [EntityStatus], newStatus: [EntityStatus]): void {\n const validTransitions: Record<[EntityStatus], [EntityStatus][]> = {\n PENDING: ['IN_PROGRESS', 'CANCELED'],\n IN_PROGRESS: ['COMPLETED', 'PENDING', 'CANCELED'],\n COMPLETED: ['IN_PROGRESS'],\n CANCELED: ['PENDING']\n }\n \n if (!validTransitions[currentStatus]?.includes(newStatus)) {\n throw new Error(`Invalid status transition from ${currentStatus} to ${newStatus}`)\n }\n}\n\nasync update[Entity]Status(\n id: string, \n status: [EntityStatus], \n userId: string\n): Promise<[Entity]WithRelations> {\n try {\n const [entity] = await this.get[Entity]ById(id, userId)\n \n // \u9a8c\u8bc1\u72b6\u6001\u8f6c\u6362\n this.validateStatusTransition([entity].status, status)\n \n // \u72b6\u6001\u7279\u5b9a\u7684\u4e1a\u52a1\u903b\u8f91\n const updateData: Update[Entity]Input = { status }\n \n if (status === 'IN_PROGRESS' && ![entity].startTime) {\n updateData.startTime = new Date()\n }\n \n if (status === 'COMPLETED') {\n updateData.endTime = new Date()\n if ([entity].startTime) {\n updateData.duration = Math.floor(\n (new Date().getTime() - [entity].startTime.getTime()) / (1000 * 60)\n )\n }\n }\n \n return await this.update[Entity](id, updateData, userId)\n } catch (error) {\n this.handleError(error, '[Entity]Service.update[Entity]Status')\n }\n}\n```\n\n### 5. \u7edf\u8ba1\u548c\u5206\u6790\u65b9\u6cd5\n\n```typescript\nasync get[Entity]Stats(userId: string): Promise<[Entity]Stats> {\n try {\n this.validateRequiredFields({ userId }, ['userId'])\n \n const stats = await this.[entity]Repository.getStats(userId)\n \n // \u6dfb\u52a0\u8ba1\u7b97\u5b57\u6bb5\n const completionRate = stats.total > 0 \n ? (stats.byStatus.COMPLETED || 0) / stats.total * 100 \n : 0\n \n return {\n ...stats,\n completionRate: Math.round(completionRate * 100) / 100\n }\n } catch (error) {\n this.handleError(error, '[Entity]Service.get[Entity]Stats')\n }\n}\n\nasync get[Entity]Trends(\n userId: string, \n startDate: Date, \n endDate: Date\n): Promise<[Entity]Trend[]> {\n try {\n this.validateRequiredFields({ userId, startDate, endDate }, ['userId', 'startDate', 'endDate'])\n \n if (startDate >= endDate) {\n throw new Error('Start date must be before end date')\n }\n \n // \u5b9e\u73b0\u8d8b\u52bf\u5206\u6790\u903b\u8f91\n const [entities] = await this.[entity]Repository.findByFilters({\n userId,\n startDate,\n endDate\n })\n \n // \u6309\u65e5\u671f\u5206\u7ec4\u7edf\u8ba1\n const trends = this.groupByDate([entities])\n \n return trends\n } catch (error) {\n this.handleError(error, '[Entity]Service.get[Entity]Trends')\n }\n}\n```\n\n### 6. \u6279\u91cf\u64cd\u4f5c\u65b9\u6cd5\n\n```typescript\nasync bulkUpdate[Entity]Status(\n ids: string[], \n status: [EntityStatus], \n userId: string\n): Promise<[Entity]WithRelations[]> {\n try {\n this.validateRequiredFields({ ids, status, userId }, ['ids', 'status', 'userId'])\n \n if (ids.length === 0) {\n throw new Error('No [entity] IDs provided')\n }\n \n if (ids.length > 100) {\n throw new Error('Cannot update more than 100 [entities] at once')\n }\n \n // \u9a8c\u8bc1\u6240\u6709\u5b9e\u4f53\u7684\u6743\u9650\n const [entities] = await Promise.all(\n ids.map(id => this.get[Entity]ById(id, userId))\n )\n \n // \u6279\u91cf\u66f4\u65b0\n const updated[Entities] = await Promise.all(\n [entities].map([entity] => \n this.update[Entity]Status([entity].id, status, userId)\n )\n )\n \n return updated[Entities]\n } catch (error) {\n this.handleError(error, '[Entity]Service.bulkUpdate[Entity]Status')\n }\n}\n```\n\n## \u547d\u540d\u89c4\u8303\n\n### \u65b9\u6cd5\u547d\u540d\n- \u521b\u5efa\uff1a`create[Entity]`\n- \u67e5\u8be2\uff1a`get[Entity]ById`, `get[Entity]List`\n- \u66f4\u65b0\uff1a`update[Entity]`, `update[Entity]Status`\n- \u5220\u9664\uff1a`delete[Entity]`\n- \u7edf\u8ba1\uff1a`get[Entity]Stats`, `get[Entity]Trends`\n- \u6279\u91cf\uff1a`bulkUpdate[Entity]Status`, `bulkDelete[Entity]`\n- \u9a8c\u8bc1\uff1a`validate[Entity]BusinessRules` (\u79c1\u6709\u65b9\u6cd5)\n\n### \u9519\u8bef\u5904\u7406\n```typescript\n// \u7edf\u4e00\u9519\u8bef\u5904\u7406\u683c\u5f0f\ntry {\n // \u4e1a\u52a1\u903b\u8f91\n} catch (error) {\n this.handleError(error, '[Entity]Service.[methodName]')\n}\n```\n\n## \u4f9d\u8d56\u6ce8\u5165\u6a21\u5f0f\n\n```typescript\nexport class [Entity]Service this.validate[Entity]BusinessRules(sanitizedData)\n \n // 4. \u6267\u884c\u521b\u5efa\u64cd\u4f5c\n const [entity] = await this.[entity]Repository.create(sanitizedData)\n \n // 5. \u540e\u7f6e\u5904\u7406\uff08\u5982\u53d1\u9001\u901a\u77e5\u3001\u8bb0\u5f55\u65e5\u5fd7\u7b49\uff09\n await this.handlePost[Entity]Creation([entity])\n \n return [entity]\n } catch (error) {\n this.handleError(error, '[Entity]Service.create[Entity]')\n }\n}\n\nprivate async validate[Entity]BusinessRules(data: Create[Entity]Input): Promise {\n // \u5b57\u6bb5\u957f\u5ea6\u9a8c\u8bc1\n if (data.[textField] && data.[textField].length > [maxLength]) {\n throw new Error('[TextField] cannot exceed [maxLength] characters')\n }\n \n // \u5173\u8054\u5b9e\u4f53\u9a8c\u8bc1\n if (data.[relationId]) {\n const relatedEntity = await this.[relatedRepository].findById(data.[relationId])\n if (!relatedEntity) {\n throw new Error('Related [entity] not found')\n }\n this.validateUserAccess(relatedEntity.userId, data.userId)\n }\n \n // \u552f\u4e00\u6027\u9a8c\u8bc1\n const existing = await this.[entity]Repository.findMany({\n where: { userId: data.userId, [uniqueField]: data.[uniqueField] }\n })\n if (existing.length > 0) {\n throw new Error('[Entity] with this [uniqueField] already exists')\n }\n}\n```\n\n#### \u67e5\u8be2\u65b9\u6cd5\u5b9e\u73b0\u6a21\u5f0f\n```typescript\nasync get[Entity]ById(id: string, userId: string): Promise<[Entity]WithRelations> {\n try {\n this.validateRequiredFields({ id, userId }, ['id', 'userId'])\n \n const [entity] = await this.[entity]Repository.findById(id)\n if (![entity]) {\n throw new Error('[Entity] not found')\n }\n \n // \u6743\u9650\u9a8c\u8bc1\n this.validateUserAccess([entity].userId, userId)\n \n return [entity]\n } catch (error) {\n this.handleError(error, '[Entity]Service.get[Entity]ById')\n }\n}\n\nasync get[Entity]List(\n filters: [Entity]Filters, \n page?: number, \n limit?: number\n): Promise> {\n try {\n const { page: validatedPage, limit: validatedLimit } = this.validatePaginationParams(page, limit)\n \n const [entities] = await this.[entity]Repository.findByFilters(filters)\n \n // \u624b\u52a8\u5206\u9875\u5904\u7406\n const startIndex = (validatedPage - 1) * validatedLimit\n const endIndex = startIndex + validatedLimit\n const paginated[Entities] = [entities].slice(startIndex, endIndex)\n \n return {\n data: paginated[Entities],\n total: [entities].length,\n page: validatedPage,\n limit: validatedLimit,\n totalPages: Math.ceil([entities].length / validatedLimit)\n }\n } catch (error) {\n this.handleError(error, '[Entity]Service.get[Entity]List')\n }\n}\n```\n\n### \u6b65\u9aa44: \u5b9e\u73b0\u72b6\u6001\u7ba1\u7406\u548c\u4e1a\u52a1\u903b\u8f91\n\n#### \u72b6\u6001\u8f6c\u6362\u9a8c\u8bc1\n```typescript\nprivate validateStatusTransition(currentStatus: [EntityStatus], newStatus: [EntityStatus]): void {\n const validTransitions: Record<[EntityStatus], [EntityStatus][]> = {\n PENDING: ['IN_PROGRESS', 'CANCELED'],\n IN_PROGRESS: ['COMPLETED', 'PENDING', 'CANCELED'],\n COMPLETED: ['IN_PROGRESS'],\n CANCELED: ['PENDING']\n }\n \n if (!validTransitions[currentStatus]?.includes(newStatus)) {\n throw new Error(`Invalid status transition from ${currentStatus} to ${newStatus}`)\n }\n}\n\nasync update[Entity]Status(\n id: string, \n status: [EntityStatus], \n userId: string\n): Promise<[Entity]WithRelations> {\n try {\n const [entity] = await this.get[Entity]ById(id, userId)\n \n // \u9a8c\u8bc1\u72b6\u6001\u8f6c\u6362\n this.validateStatusTransition([entity].status, status)\n \n // \u72b6\u6001\u7279\u5b9a\u7684\u4e1a\u52a1\u903b\u8f91\n const updateData: Update[Entity]Input = { status }\n \n // \u81ea\u52a8\u8bbe\u7f6e\u65f6\u95f4\u6233\n if (status === 'IN_PROGRESS' && ![entity].startTime) {\n updateData.startTime = new Date()\n }\n \n if (status === 'COMPLETED') {\n updateData.endTime = new Date()\n if ([entity].startTime) {\n updateData.duration = Math.floor(\n (new Date().getTime() - [entity].startTime.getTime()) / (1000 * 60)\n )\n }\n }\n \n return await this.update[Entity](id, updateData, userId)\n } catch (error) {\n this.handleError(error, '[Entity]Service.update[Entity]Status')\n }\n}\n```\n\n### \u6b65\u9aa45: \u5b9e\u73b0\u7edf\u8ba1\u548c\u5206\u6790\u529f\u80fd\n\n#### \u7edf\u8ba1\u65b9\u6cd5\u5b9e\u73b0\u6a21\u5f0f\n```typescript\nasync get[Entity]Stats(userId: string): Promise<[Entity]Stats> {\n try {\n this.validateRequiredFields({ userId }, ['userId'])\n \n const baseStats = await this.[entity]Repository.getStats(userId)\n \n // \u8ba1\u7b97\u884d\u751f\u6307\u6807\n const completionRate = baseStats.total > 0 \n ? (baseStats.byStatus.COMPLETED || 0) / baseStats.total * 100 \n : 0\n \n const activeCount = (baseStats.byStatus.PENDING || 0) + (baseStats.byStatus.IN_PROGRESS || 0)\n \n return {\n ...baseStats,\n completionRate: Math.round(completionRate * 100) / 100,\n activeCount,\n productivity: this.calculateProductivity(baseStats)\n }\n } catch (error) {\n this.handleError(error, '[Entity]Service.get[Entity]Stats')\n }\n}\n\nasync get[Entity]Trends(\n userId: string, \n startDate: Date, \n endDate: Date\n): Promise<[Entity]Trend[]> {\n try {\n this.validateRequiredFields({ userId, startDate, endDate }, ['userId', 'startDate', 'endDate'])\n \n if (startDate >= endDate) {\n throw new Error('Start date must be before end date')\n }\n \n const [entities] = await this.[entity]Repository.findByFilters({\n userId,\n startDate,\n endDate\n })\n \n // \u6309\u65e5\u671f\u5206\u7ec4\u7edf\u8ba1\n return this.groupByDateAndCalculateTrends([entities])\n } catch (error) {\n this.handleError(error, '[Entity]Service.get[Entity]Trends')\n }\n}\n```\n\n### \u6b65\u9aa46: \u5b9e\u73b0\u6279\u91cf\u64cd\u4f5c\n\n#### \u6279\u91cf\u64cd\u4f5c\u5b9e\u73b0\u6a21\u5f0f\n```typescript\nasync bulkUpdate[Entity]Status(\n ids: string[], \n status: [EntityStatus], \n userId: string\n): Promise<[Entity]WithRelations[]> {\n try {\n this.validateRequiredFields({ ids, status, userId }, ['ids', 'status', 'userId'])\n \n if (ids.length === 0) {\n throw new Error('No [entity] IDs provided')\n }\n \n if (ids.length > 100) {\n throw new Error('Cannot update more than 100 [entities] at once')\n }\n \n // \u9a8c\u8bc1\u6240\u6709\u5b9e\u4f53\u7684\u5b58\u5728\u6027\u548c\u6743\u9650\n const [entities] = await Promise.all(\n ids.map(id => this.get[Entity]ById(id, userId))\n )\n \n // \u9a8c\u8bc1\u6240\u6709\u72b6\u6001\u8f6c\u6362\u7684\u6709\u6548\u6027\n [entities].forEach([entity] => {\n this.validateStatusTransition([entity].status, status)\n })\n \n // \u6267\u884c\u6279\u91cf\u66f4\u65b0\n const updated[Entities] = await Promise.all(\n [entities].map([entity] => \n this.update[Entity]Status([entity].id, status, userId)\n )\n )\n \n return updated[Entities]\n } catch (error) {\n this.handleError(error, '[Entity]Service.bulkUpdate[Entity]Status')\n }\n}\n```"}","mcp_server_name":"","mcp_tool_name":""}},{"id":0,"type":0,"content":"现在让我生成Service层的代码生成规则： \u590d\u6742\u64cd\u4f5c\u9700\u8981\u8003\u8651\u4e8b\u52a1\u4e00\u81f4\u6027\n\n### \u4e1a\u52a1\u89c4\u5219\u5b9e\u73b0\u6307\u5bfc\n- **\u72b6\u6001\u7ba1\u7406**: \u5b9e\u73b0\u72b6\u6001\u673a\u6a21\u5f0f\uff0c\u786e\u4fdd\u72b6\u6001\u8f6c\u6362\u7684\u5408\u6cd5\u6027\n- **\u6570\u636e\u5b8c\u6574\u6027**: \u9a8c\u8bc1\u5173\u8054\u6570\u636e\u7684\u5b58\u5728\u6027\u548c\u4e00\u81f4\u6027\n- **\u4e1a\u52a1\u7ea6\u675f**: \u5b9e\u73b0\u4e1a\u52a1\u7279\u5b9a\u7684\u9a8c\u8bc1\u89c4\u5219\n- **\u5ba1\u8ba1\u65e5\u5fd7**: \u8bb0\u5f55\u91cd\u8981\u4e1a\u52a1\u64cd\u4f5c\u7684\u65e5\u5fd7\n\n### \u6027\u80fd\u4f18\u5316\u8981\u6c42\n- **\u6279\u91cf\u64cd\u4f5c**: \u63d0\u4f9b\u6279\u91cf\u5904\u7406\u529f\u80fd\uff0c\u4f46\u9650\u5236\u6279\u91cf\u5927\u5c0f\n- **\u7f13\u5b58\u7b56\u7565**: \u5bf9\u9891\u7e41\u67e5\u8be2\u7684\u6570\u636e\u5b9e\u73b0\u7f13\u5b58\n- **\u5f02\u6b65\u5904\u7406**: \u8017\u65f6\u64cd\u4f5c\u4f7f\u7528\u5f02\u6b65\u5904\u7406\n- **\u5206\u9875\u67e5\u8be2**: \u5927\u6570\u636e\u91cf\u67e5\u8be2\u5fc5\u987b\u652f\u6301\u5206\u9875\n\n## \u793a\u4f8b\u751f\u6210\u5bf9\u8bdd\n\n**\u7528\u6237**: \u4ee3\u7801\u751f\u6210\u6a21\u677f\n\n### \u6b65\u9aa41: \u5206\u6790API\u9700\u6c42\n\u9996\u5148\u5206\u6790\u5b9e\u4f53\u7684API\u9700\u6c42\uff1a\n- CRUD\u64cd\u4f5c\u7684HTTP\u65b9\u6cd5\u6620\u5c04\n- \u8def\u7531\u53c2\u6570\u548c\u67e5\u8be2\u53c2\u6570\n- \u8bf7\u6c42\u4f53\u7ed3\u6784\n- \u54cd\u5e94\u6570\u636e\u683c\u5f0f\n- \u9519\u8bef\u5904\u7406\u9700\u6c42\n\n### \u6b65\u9aa42: \u8bbe\u8ba1\u8def\u7531\u7ed3\u6784\n```\nsrc/app/api/[entities]/\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 route.ts # GET /api/[entities] (\u5217\u8868), POST /api/[entities] (\u521b\u5efa)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 [id]/\n\u2502 \u2514\u2500\u2500 route.ts # GET /api/[entities]/[id] (\u8be6\u60c5), PUT /api/[entities]/[id] (\u66f4\u65b0), DELETE /api/[entities]/[id] (\u5220\u9664)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 stats/\n\u2502 \u2514\u2500\u2500 route.ts # GET /api/[entities]/stats (\u7edf\u8ba1)\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 bulk/\n \u2514\u2500\u2500 route.ts # POST /api/[entities]/bulk (\u6279\u91cf\u64cd\u4f5c)\n```\n\n### \u6b65\u9aa43: \u751f\u6210\u4e3b\u8def\u7531\u6587\u4ef6 (route.ts)\n\n#### \u5217\u8868\u67e5\u8be2\u548c\u521b\u5efa\u63a5\u53e3\n```typescript\nimport { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server'\nimport { [Entity]Service } from '@/lib/services/[entity].service'\nimport { [Entity]Filters } from '@/lib/repositories/[entity].repository'\n\nconst [entity]Service = new [Entity]Service()\n\n// GET /api/[entities] - \u83b7\u53d6[\u5b9e\u4f53]\u5217\u8868\nexport async function GET(request: NextRequest) {\n try {\n // 1. \u89e3\u6790\u67e5\u8be2\u53c2\u6570\n const { searchParams } = new URL(request.url)\n const userId = searchParams.get('userId')\n const status = searchParams.get('status')\n const [relationId] = searchParams.get('[relationId]')\n const search = searchParams.get('search')\n const page = parseInt(searchParams.get('page') || '1')\n const limit = parseInt(searchParams.get('limit') || '10')\n const startDate = searchParams.get('startDate') ? new Date(searchParams.get('startDate')!) : undefined\n const endDate = searchParams.get('endDate') ? new Date(searchParams.get('endDate')!) : undefined\n\n // 2. \u53c2\u6570\u9a8c\u8bc1\n if (!userId) {\n return NextResponse.json(\n { error: 'userId is required' },\n { status: 400 }\n )\n }\n\n // 3. \u6784\u5efa\u8fc7\u6ee4\u6761\u4ef6\n const filters: [Entity]Filters = {\n userId,\n ...(status && { status: status as [EntityStatus] }),\n ...([relationId] && { [relationId] }),\n ...(search && { search }),\n ...(startDate && endDate && { startDate, endDate })\n }\n\n // 4. \u8c03\u7528Service\u5c42\n const result = await [entity]Service.get[Entity]List(filters, page, limit)\n\n // 5. \u8fd4\u56de\u6210\u529f\u54cd\u5e94\n return NextResponse.json({\n success: true,\n data: result.data,\n pagination: {\n page: result.page,\n limit: result.limit,\n total: result.total,\n totalPages: result.totalPages\n }\n })\n\n } catch (error) {\n console.error('GET /api/[entities] error:', error)\n return NextResponse.json(\n { \n success: false,\n error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Internal server error' \n },\n { status: 500 }\n )\n }\n}\n\n// POST /api/[entities] - \u521b\u5efa[\u5b9e\u4f53]\nexport async function POST(request: NextRequest) {\n try {\n // 1. \u89e3\u6790\u8bf7\u6c42\u4f53\n const body = await request.json()\n \n // 2. \u57fa\u7840\u9a8c\u8bc1\n if (!body.userId) {\n return NextResponse.json(\n { error: 'userId is required' },\n { status: 400 }\n )\n }\n\n if (!body.[requiredField]) {\n return NextResponse.json(\n { error: '[requiredField] is required' },\n { status: 400 }\n )\n }\n\n // 3. \u8c03\u7528Service\u5c42\n const [entity] = await [entity]Service.create[Entity](body)\n\n // 4. \u8fd4\u56de\u6210\u529f\u54cd\u5e94\n return NextResponse.json({\n success: true,\n data: [entity]\n }, { status: 201 })\n\n } catch (error) {\n console.error('POST /api/[entities] error:', error)\n \n // \u4e1a\u52a1\u903b\u8f91\u9519\u8bef\u8fd4\u56de400\uff0c\u7cfb\u7edf\u9519\u8bef\u8fd4\u56de500\n const statusCode = error instanceof Error && error.message.includes('already exists') ? 400 : 500\n \n return NextResponse.json(\n { \n success: false,\n error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Internal server error' \n },\n { status: statusCode }\n )\n }\n}\n```\n\n### \u6b65\u9aa44: \u751f\u6210\u8be6\u60c5\u8def\u7531\u6587\u4ef6 ([id]/route.ts)\n\n#### \u8be6\u60c5\u67e5\u8be2\u3001\u66f4\u65b0\u548c\u5220\u9664\u63a5\u53e3\n```typescript\nimport { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server'\nimport { [Entity]Service } from '@/lib/services/[entity].service'\n\nconst [entity]Service = new [Entity]Service()\n\n// GET /api/[entities]/[id] - \u83b7\u53d6[\u5b9e\u4f53]\u8be6\u60c5\nexport async function GET(\n request: NextRequest,\n { params }: { params: { id: string } }\n) {\n try {\n const { searchParams } = new URL(request.url)\n const userId = searchParams.get('userId')\n\n if (!userId) {\n return NextResponse.json(\n { error: 'userId is required' },\n { status: 400 }\n )\n }\n\n const [entity] = await [entity]Service.get[Entity]ById(params.id, userId)\n\n return NextResponse.json({\n success: true,\n data: [entity]\n })\n\n } catch (error) {\n console.error(`GET /api/[entities]/${params.id} error:`, error)\n \n const statusCode = error instanceof Error && error.message.includes('not found') ? 404 : 500\n \n return NextResponse.json(\n { \n success: false,\n error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Internal server error' \n },\n { status: statusCode }\n )\n }\n}\n\n// PUT /api/[entities]/[id] - \u66f4\u65b0[\u5b9e\u4f53]\nexport async function PUT(\n request: NextRequest,\n { params }: { params: { id: string } }\n) {\n try {\n const body = await request.json()\n const { userId, ...updateData } = body\n\n if (!userId) {\n return NextResponse.json(\n { error: 'userId is required' },\n { status: 400 }\n )\n }\n\n const [entity] = await [entity]Service.update[Entity](params.id, updateData, userId)\n\n return NextResponse.json({\n success: true,\n data: [entity]\n })\n\n } catch (error) {\n console.error(`PUT /api/[entities]/${params.id} error:`, error)\n \n let statusCode = 500\n if (error instanceof Error) {\n if (error.message.includes('not found')) statusCode = 404\n if (error.message.includes('Access denied')) statusCode = 403\n if (error.message.includes('Invalid')) statusCode = 400\n }\n \n return NextResponse.json(\n { \n success: false,\n error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Internal server error' \n },\n { status: statusCode }\n )\n }\n}\n\n// DELETE /api/[entities]/[id] - \u5220\u9664[\u5b9e\u4f53]\nexport async function DELETE(\n request: NextRequest,\n { params }: { params: { id: string } }\n) {\n try {\n const { searchParams } = new URL(request.url)\n const userId = searchParams.get('userId')\n\n if (!userId) {\n return NextResponse.json(\n { error: 'userId is required' },\n { status: 400 }\n )\n }\n\n const success = await [entity]Service.delete[Entity](params.id, userId)\n\n if (!success) {\n return NextResponse.json(\n { error: '[Entity] not found' },\n { status: 404 }\n )\n }\n\n return NextResponse.json({\n success: true,\n message: '[Entity] deleted successfully'\n })\n\n } catch (error) {\n console.error(`DELETE /api/[entities]/${params.id} error:`, error)\n \n let statusCode = 500\n if (error instanceof Error) {\n if (error.message.includes('not found')) statusCode = 404\n if (error.message.includes('Access denied')) statusCode = 403\n if (error.message.includes('Cannot delete')) statusCode = 400\n }\n \n return NextResponse.json(\n { \n success: false,\n error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Internal server error' \n },\n { status: statusCode }\n )\n }\n}\n```\n\n### \u6b65\u9aa45: \u751f\u6210\u7edf\u8ba1\u8def\u7531\u6587\u4ef6 (stats/route.ts)\n\n#### \u7edf\u8ba1\u6570\u636e\u63a5\u53e3\n```typescript\nimport { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server'\nimport { [Entity]Service } from '@/lib/services/[entity].service'\n\nconst [entity]Service = new [Entity]Service()\n\n// GET /api/[entities]/stats - \u83b7\u53d6[\u5b9e\u4f53]\u7edf\u8ba1\u6570\u636e\nexport async function GET(request: NextRequest) {\n try {\n const { searchParams } = new URL(request.url)\n const userId = searchParams.get('userId')\n const type = searchParams.get('type') || 'overview'\n const startDate = searchParams.get('startDate') ? new Date(searchParams.get('startDate')!) : undefined\n const endDate = searchParams.get('endDate') ? new Date(searchParams.get('endDate')!) : undefined\n\n if (!userId) {\n return NextResponse.json(\n { error: 'userId is required' },\n { status: 400 }\n )\n }\n\n let data\n switch (type) {\n case 'overview':\n data = await [entity]Service.get[Entity]Stats(userId)\n break\n case 'trends':\n if (!startDate || !endDate) {\n return NextResponse.json(\n { error: 'startDate and endDate are required for trends' },\n { status: 400 }\n )\n }\n data = await [entity]Service.get[Entity]Trends(userId, startDate, endDate)\n break\n default:\n return NextResponse.json(\n { error: 'Invalid stats type' },\n { status: 400 }\n )\n }\n\n return NextResponse.json({\n success: true,\n data\n })\n\n } catch (error) {\n console.error('GET /api/[entities]/stats error:', error)\n return NextResponse.json(\n { \n success: false,\n error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Internal server error' \n },\n { status: 500 }\n )\n }\n}\n```"}","mcp_server_name":"","mcp_tool_name":""}},{"id":0,"type":0,"content":"现在生成Controller层（API路由）的代码生成规则： **\u52a8\u753b**: framer-motion\n- **\u56fe\u6807**: lucide-react\n- **\u8868\u5355**: react-hook-form + zod\u9a8c\u8bc1\n- **\u56fe\u8868**: recharts\n- **\u6837\u5f0f**: Tailwind CSS (\u7d27\u51d1\u5e03\u5c40\u8bbe\u8ba1)\n- **\u7c7b\u578b**: TypeScript\u4e25\u683c\u6a21\u5f0f\n\n## \u4ee3\u7801\u751f\u6210\u6a21\u677f\n\n### \u6b65\u9aa41: \u5206\u6790\u7ec4\u4ef6\u9700\u6c42\n\u9996\u5148\u5206\u6790\u7ec4\u4ef6\u7684\u529f\u80fd\u9700\u6c42\uff1a\n- \u7ec4\u4ef6\u7c7b\u578b\uff08\u8868\u5355\u3001\u5217\u8868\u3001\u5361\u7247\u3001\u56fe\u8868\u7b49\uff09\n- \u6570\u636e\u7ed3\u6784\u548c\u63a5\u53e3\n- \u751f\u6210\u7ec4\u4ef6\u57fa\u7840\u7ed3\u6784\n\n#### \u7ec4\u4ef6\u6587\u4ef6\u7ed3\u6784\n```\nsrc/components/[domain]/\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 [Entity]Card.tsx # \u5b9e\u4f53\u5361\u7247\u7ec4\u4ef6\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 [Entity]Form.tsx # \u5b9e\u4f53\u8868\u5355\u7ec4\u4ef6\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 [Entity]List.tsx # \u5b9e\u4f53\u56fe\u8868\u7ec4\u4ef6\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 index.ts # \u5bfc\u51fa\u6587\u4ef6\n```\n\n#### \u57fa\u7840\u7ec4\u4ef6\u6a21\u677f\n```typescript\n'use client'\n\nimport { useState } from 'react'\nimport { motion, AnimatePresence } from 'framer-motion'\nimport { [Icon1], [Icon2], [Icon3] } from 'lucide-react'\nimport { useForm } from 'react-hook-form'\nimport { zodResolver } from '@hookform/resolvers/zod'\nimport { z } from 'zod'\n\n// \u7c7b\u578b\u5b9a\u4e49\ninterface [Component]Props {\n [entity]?: [EntityType]\n onSubmit?: (data: [FormData]) => void\n onEdit?: (id: string) => void\n onDelete?: (id: string) => void\n loading?: boolean\n className?: string\n}\n\n// Zod\u9a8c\u8bc1\u6a21\u5f0f\nconst [entity]Schema = z.object({\n [field1]: z.string().min(1, '[Field1] is required').max(100, '[Field1] is too long'),\n [field2]: z.string().optional(),\n [enumField]: z.enum(['VALUE1', 'VALUE2', 'VALUE3']),\n [numberField]: z.number().min(0, 'Must be positive').optional()\n})\n\ntype [Entity]FormData = z.infer\n\nexport function [Component]({ [entity], onSubmit, onEdit, onDelete, loading, className }: [Component]Props) {\n // \u7ec4\u4ef6\u5b9e\u73b0...\n}\n```\n\n### \u6b65\u9aa43: \u5b9e\u73b0\u8868\u5355\u7ec4\u4ef6\n\n#### \u8868\u5355\u7ec4\u4ef6\u5b9e\u73b0\u6a21\u5f0f\n```typescript\nexport function [Entity]Form({ [entity], onSubmit, loading, className }: [Entity]FormProps) {\n const {\n register,\n handleSubmit,\n formState: { errors, isSubmitting },\n reset,\n watch\n } = useForm<[Entity]FormData>({\n resolver: zodResolver([entity]Schema),\n defaultValues: [entity] ? {\n [field1]: [entity].[field1],\n [field2]: [entity].[field2] || '',\n [enumField]: [entity].[enumField],\n [numberField]: [entity].[numberField]\n } : undefined\n })\n\n const handleFormSubmit = async (data: [Entity]FormData) => {\n try {\n await onSubmit?.(data)\n if (![entity]) {\n reset() // \u521b\u5efa\u6210\u529f\u540e\u91cd\u7f6e\u8868\u5355\n }\n } catch (error) {\n console.error('Form submission error:', error)\n }\n }\n\n return (\n \n
\n

\n <[Icon] className=\"w-5 h-5\" />\n {[entity] ? 'Edit [Entity]' : 'Create [Entity]'}\n

\n\n
\n {/* \u6587\u672c\u8f93\u5165\u5b57\u6bb5 */}\n
\n \n \n {errors.[field1] && (\n \n )}\n

\n reset()}\n disabled={isSubmitting || loading}\n >\n Reset\n \n \n {isSubmitting || loading ? (\n <>\n \n {[entity] ? 'Updating...' : 'Creating...'}\n \n ) : (\n <>\n <[SubmitIcon] className=\"w-4 h-4\" />\n {[entity] ? 'Update' : 'Create'}\n \n )}\n \n

\n {/* \u5217\u8868\u5934\u90e8 */}\n
\n

\n <[ListIcon] className=\"w-5 h-5\" />\n [Entities] ({[entities].length})\n

\n \n {selectedItems.length > 0 && (\n
\n \n {selectedItems.length} selected\n \n {\n selectedItems.forEach(id => onDelete?.(id))\n setSelectedItems([])\n }}\n >\n <[DeleteIcon] className=\"w-3 h-3\" />\n Delete\n \n
\n )}\n
\n\n {/* \u5217\u8868\u5185\u5bb9 */}\n
\n \n {sorted[Entities].map(([entity], index) => (\n \n {/* \u9009\u62e9\u6846 */}\n handleSelectItem([entity].id)}\n />\n\n {/* \u72b6\u6001\u6307\u793a\u5668 */}\n

Updates\u63d0\u4f9b\u5373\u65f6UI\u53cd\u9988\n4. \u521b\u5efaContext Provider\u7edf\u4e00\u6570\u636e\u8bbf\u95ee\n5. \u786e\u4fdd\u4e09\u5c42\u4e4b\u95f4\u7684\u65e0\u7f1d\u96c6\u6210\n\n## \u6280\u672f\u4e0a\u4e0b\u6587\n- **Layer 1**: Server Components (\u521d\u59cb\u6570\u636e\u83b7\u53d6)\n- **Layer 2**: React Query (\u5ba2\u6237\u7aef\u7f13\u5b58\u548c\u66f4\u65b0)\n- **Layer 3**: Optimistic Updates (\u5373\u65f6UI\u53cd\u9988)\n- **\u72b6\u6001\u7ba1\u7406**: TanStack Query v5\n- **\u6846\u67b6**: Next.js 15 App Router\n- **\u7c7b\u578b\u5b89\u5168**: TypeScript\u4e25\u683c\u6a21\u5f0f\n\n## \u4e09\u5c42\u67b6\u6784\u6a21\u5f0f\n\n### Layer 1: Server Components (\u670d\u52a1\u7aef\u6570\u636e\u83b7\u53d6)\n```typescript\n// src/lib/data/[entity].data.ts\nimport { [Entity]Service } from '@/lib/services/[entity].service'\nimport { [Entity]Filters } from '@/lib/repositories/[entity].repository'\n\nconst [entity]Service = new [Entity]Service()\n\n// \u670d\u52a1\u7aef\u6570\u636e\u83b7\u53d6\u51fd\u6570\nexport async function getAll[Entities](filters: [Entity]Filters) {\n 'use server'\n \n try {\n const result = await [entity]Service.get[Entity]List(filters)\n return {\n success: true,\n data: result.data,\n pagination: {\n page: result.page,\n limit: result.limit,\n total: result.total,\n totalPages: result.totalPages\n }\n }\n } catch (error) {\n console.error('Server data fetch error:', error)\n return {\n success: false,\n error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Failed to fetch data'\n }\n }\n}\n\nexport async function get[Entity]ById(id: string, userId: string) {\n 'use server'\n \n try {\n const [entity] = await [entity]Service.get[Entity]ById(id, userId)\n return {\n success: true,\n data: [entity]\n }\n } catch (error) {\n console.error('Server data fetch error:', error)\n return {\n success: false,\n error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Failed to fetch data'\n }\n }\n}\n\nexport async function get[Entity]Stats(userId: string) {\n 'use server'\n \n try {\n const stats = await [entity]Service.get[Entity]Stats(userId)\n return {\n success: true,\n data: stats\n }\n } catch (error) {\n console.error('Server stats fetch error:', error)\n return {\n success: false,\n error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Failed to fetch stats'\n }\n }\n}\n```\n\n### Layer 2: React Query Hooks (\u5ba2\u6237\u7aef\u72b6\u6001\u7ba1\u7406)\n```typescript\n// src/hooks/use[Entities].ts\n'use client'\n\nimport { useQuery, useMutation, useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'\nimport { [Entity]WithRelations, [Entity]Filters, Create[Entity]Input, Update[Entity]Input } from '@/lib/repositories/[entity].repository'\n\n// Query Keys\nexport const [entity]Keys = {\n all: ['[entities]'] as const,\n lists: () => [...[entity]Keys.all, 'list'] as const,\n list: (filters: [Entity]Filters) => [...[entity]Keys.lists(), filters] as const,\n details: () => [...[entity]Keys.all, 'detail'] as const,\n detail: (id: string) => [...[entity]Keys.details(), id] as const,\n stats: (userId: string) => [...[entity]Keys.all, 'stats', userId] as const,\n}\n\n// API\u8c03\u7528\u51fd\u6570\nasync function fetch[Entities](filters: [Entity]Filters): Promise<{\n data: [Entity]WithRelations[]\n pagination: PaginationInfo\n}> {\n const params = new URLSearchParams({\n userId: filters.userId,\n ...(filters.status && { status: filters.status }),\n ...(filters.search && { search: filters.search }),\n ...(filters.startDate && { startDate: filters.startDate.toISOString() }),\n ...(filters.endDate && { endDate: filters.endDate.toISOString() }),\n page: filters.page?.toString() || '1',\n limit: filters.limit?.toString() || '10'\n })\n\n const response = await fetch(`/api/[entities]?${params}`)\n if (!response.ok) {\n throw new Error('Failed to fetch [entities]')\n }\n \n const result = await response.json()\n if (!result.success) {\n throw new Error(result.error)\n }\n \n return {\n data: result.data,\n pagination: result.pagination\n }\n}\n\nasync function fetch[Entity]ById(id: string, userId: string): Promise<[Entity]WithRelations> {\n const response = await fetch(`/api/[entities]/${id}?userId=${userId}`)\n if (!response.ok) {\n throw new Error('Failed to fetch [entity]')\n }\n \n const result = await response.json()\n if (!result.success) {\n throw new Error(result.error)\n }\n \n return result.data\n}\n\nasync function create[Entity](data: Create[Entity]Input): Promise<[Entity]WithRelations> {\n const response = await fetch('/api/[entities]', {\n method: 'POST',\n headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n body: JSON.stringify(data)\n })\n \n if (!response.ok) {\n throw new Error('Failed to create [entity]')\n }\n \n const result = await response.json()\n if (!result.success) {\n throw new Error(result.error)\n }\n \n return result.data\n}\n\nasync function update[Entity](id: string, data: Update[Entity]Input & { userId: string }): Promise<[Entity]WithRelations> {\n const response = await fetch(`/api/[entities]/${id}`, {\n method: 'PUT',\n headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n body: JSON.stringify(data)\n })\n \n if (!response.ok) {\n throw new Error('Failed to update [entity]')\n }\n \n const result = await response.json()\n if (!result.success) {\n throw new Error(result.error)\n }\n \n return result.data\n}\n\nasync function delete[Entity](id: string, userId: string): Promise {\n const response = await fetch(`/api/[entities]/${id}?userId=${userId}`, {\n method: 'DELETE'\n })\n \n if (!response.ok) {\n throw new Error('Failed to delete [entity]')\n }\n \n const result = await response.json()\n if (!result.success) {\n throw new Error(result.error)\n }\n}\n\n// React Query Hooks\nexport function use[Entities](filters: [Entity]Filters, initialData?: [Entity]WithRelations[]) {\n return useQuery({\n queryKey: [entity]Keys.list(filters),\n queryFn: () => fetch[Entities](filters),\n initialData: initialData ? { data: initialData, pagination: { page: 1, limit: 10, total: initialData.length, totalPages: 1 } } : undefined,\n staleTime: 5 * 60 * 1000, // 5\u5206\u949f\n gcTime: 10 * 60 * 1000, // 10\u5206\u949f\n })\n}\n\nexport function use[Entity](id: string, userId: string, initialData?: [Entity]WithRelations) {\n return useQuery({\n queryKey: [entity]Keys.detail(id),\n queryFn: () => fetch[Entity]ById(id, userId),\n initialData,\n enabled: !!id && !!userId,\n staleTime: 5 * 60 * 1000,\n gcTime: 10 * 60 * 1000,\n })\n}\n\nexport function use[Entity]Stats(userId: string, initialData?: [Entity]Stats) {\n return useQuery({\n queryKey: [entity]Keys.stats(userId),\n queryFn: async () => {\n const response = await fetch(`/api/[entities]/stats?userId=${userId}`)\n if (!response.ok) throw new Error('Failed to fetch stats')\n const result = await response.json()\n if (!result.success) throw new Error(result.error)\n return result.data\n },\n initialData,\n enabled: !!userId,\n staleTime: 2 * 60 * 1000, // 2\u5206\u949f\n gcTime: 5 * 60 * 1000,\n })\n}\n```\n\n### Layer 3: Optimistic Updates (\u5373\u65f6UI\u53cd\u9988)\n```typescript\n// src/hooks/use[Entity]Mutations.ts\n'use client'\n\nimport { useMutation, useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'\nimport { [entity]Keys } from './use[Entities]'\nimport { [Entity]WithRelations, Create[Entity]Input, Update[Entity]Input, [EntityStatus] } from '@/lib/repositories/[entity].repository'\n\nexport function use[Entity]Mutations() {\n const queryClient = useQueryClient()\n\n // \u521b\u5efa[\u5b9e\u4f53]\n const create[Entity]Mutation = useMutation({\n mutationFn: create[Entity],\n onMutate: async (newData: Create[Entity]Input) => {\n // \u53d6\u6d88\u76f8\u5173\u7684\u67e5\u8be2\u4ee5\u907f\u514d\u51b2\u7a81\n await queryClient.cancelQueries({ queryKey: [entity]Keys.lists() })\n \n // \u83b7\u53d6\u5f53\u524d\u6570\u636e\u5feb\u7167\n const previousData = queryClient.getQueriesData({ queryKey: [entity]Keys.lists() })\n \n // \u4e50\u89c2\u66f4\u65b0\uff1a\u6dfb\u52a0\u65b0\u9879\u76ee\u5230\u5217\u8868\n queryClient.setQueriesData(\n { queryKey: [entity]Keys.lists() },\n (old: any) => {\n if (!old) return old\n \n const optimistic[Entity]: [Entity]WithRelations = {\n id: `temp-${Date.now()}`,\n ...newData,\n createdAt: new Date(),\n updatedAt: new Date(),\n // \u6dfb\u52a0\u5176\u4ed6\u5fc5\u9700\u5b57\u6bb5\u7684\u9ed8\u8ba4\u503c\n }\n \n return {\n ...old,\n data: [optimistic[Entity], ...old.data],\n pagination: {\n ...old.pagination,\n total: old.pagination.total + 1\n }\n }\n }\n )\n \n return { previousData }\n },\n onError: (error, variables, context) => {\n // \u56de\u6eda\u4e50\u89c2\u66f4\u65b0\n if (context?.previousData) {\n context.previousData.forEach(([queryKey, data]) => {\n queryClient.setQueryData(queryKey, data)\n })\n }\n },\n onSuccess: (data, variables) => {\n // \u66f4\u65b0\u76f8\u5173\u67e5\u8be2\n queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: [entity]Keys.lists() })\n queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: [entity]Keys.stats(variables.userId) })\n }\n })\n\n // \u66f4\u65b0[\u5b9e\u4f53]\n const update[Entity]Mutation = useMutation({\n mutationFn: ({ id, data }: { id: string; data: Update[Entity]Input & { userId: string } }) => \n update[Entity](id, data),\n onMutate: async ({ id, data }) => {\n await queryClient.cancelQueries({ queryKey: [entity]Keys.detail(id) })\n await queryClient.cancelQueries({ queryKey: [entity]Keys.lists() })\n \n const previousDetail = queryClient.getQueryData([entity]Keys.detail(id))\n const previousLists = queryClient.getQueriesData({ queryKey: [entity]Keys.lists() })\n \n // \u4e50\u89c2\u66f4\u65b0\u8be6\u60c5\n queryClient.setQueryData([entity]Keys.detail(id), (old: [Entity]WithRelations) => ({\n ...old,\n ...data,\n updatedAt: new Date()\n }))\n \n // \u4e50\u89c2\u66f4\u65b0\u5217\u8868\u4e2d\u7684\u9879\u76ee\n queryClient.setQueriesData(\n { queryKey: [entity]Keys.lists() },\n (old: any) => {\n if (!old) return old\n \n return {\n ...old,\n data: old.data.map((item: [Entity]WithRelations) =>\n item.id === id ? { ...item, ...data, updatedAt: new Date() } : item\n )\n }\n }\n )\n \n return { previousDetail, previousLists }\n },\n onError: (error, { id }, context) => {\n if (context?.previousDetail) {\n queryClient.setQueryData([entity]Keys.detail(id), context.previousDetail)\n }\n if (context?.previousLists) {\n context.previousLists.forEach(([queryKey, data]) => {\n queryClient.setQueryData(queryKey, data)\n })\n }\n },\n onSuccess: (data, { id, data: updateData }) => {\n queryClient.setQueryData([entity]Keys.detail(id), data)\n queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: [entity]Keys.lists() })\n queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: [entity]Keys.stats(updateData.userId) })\n }\n })\n\n // \u5220\u9664[\u5b9e\u4f53]\n const delete[Entity]Mutation = useMutation({\n mutationFn: ({ id, userId }: { id: string; userId: string }) => delete[Entity](id, userId),\n onMutate: async ({ id, userId }) => {\n await queryClient.cancelQueries({ queryKey: [entity]Keys.lists() })\n \n const previousLists = queryClient.getQueriesData({ queryKey: [entity]Keys.lists() })\n \n // \u4e50\u89c2\u5220\u9664\uff1a\u4ece\u5217\u8868\u4e2d\u79fb\u9664\u9879\u76ee\n queryClient.setQueriesData(\n { queryKey: [entity]Keys.lists() },\n (old: any) => {\n if (!old) return old\n \n return {\n ...old,\n data: old.data.filter((item: [Entity]WithRelations) => item.id !== id),\n pagination: {\n ...old.pagination,\n total: old.pagination.total - 1\n }\n }\n }\n )\n \n return { previousLists }\n },\n onError: (error, { id }, context) => {\n if (context?.previousLists) {\n context.previousLists.forEach(([queryKey, data]) => {\n queryClient.setQueryData(queryKey, data)\n })\n }\n },\n onSuccess: (data, { userId }) => {\n queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: [entity]Keys.lists() })\n queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: [entity]Keys.stats(userId) })\n }\n })\n\n // \u66f4\u65b0\u72b6\u6001\uff08\u7279\u6b8a\u7684\u66f4\u65b0\u64cd\u4f5c\uff09\n const updateStatus = useMutation({\n mutationFn: ({ id, status, userId }: { id: string; status: [EntityStatus]; userId: string }) =>\n update[Entity](id, { status, userId }),\n onMutate: async ({ id, status }) => {\n await queryClient.cancelQueries({ queryKey: [entity]Keys.detail(id) })\n await queryClient.cancelQueries({ queryKey: [entity]Keys.lists() })\n \n const previousDetail = queryClient.getQueryData([entity]Keys.detail(id))\n const previousLists = queryClient.getQueriesData({ queryKey: [entity]Keys.lists() })\n \n // \u4e50\u89c2\u66f4\u65b0\u72b6\u6001\n const updateData = { status, updatedAt: new Date() }\n \n queryClient.setQueryData([entity]Keys.detail(id), (old: [Entity]WithRelations) => ({\n ...old,\n ...updateData\n }))\n \n queryClient.setQueriesData(\n { queryKey: [entity]Keys.lists() },\n (old: any) => {\n if (!old) return old\n \n return {\n ...old,\n data: old.data.map((item: [Entity]WithRelations) =>\n item.id === id ? { ...item, ...updateData } : item\n )\n }\n }\n )\n \n return { previousDetail, previousLists }\n },\n onError: (error, { id }, context) => {\n if (context?.previousDetail) {\n queryClient.setQueryData([entity]Keys.detail(id), context.previousDetail)\n }\n if (context?.previousLists) {\n context.previousLists.forEach(([queryKey, data]) => {\n queryClient.setQueryData(queryKey, data)\n })\n }\n },\n onSuccess: (data, { id, userId }) => {\n queryClient.setQueryData([entity]Keys.detail(id), data)\n queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: [entity]Keys.lists() })\n queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: [entity]Keys.stats(userId) })\n }\n })\n\n return {\n create[Entity]: create[Entity]Mutation,\n update[Entity]: update[Entity]Mutation,\n delete[Entity]: delete[Entity]Mutation,\n updateStatus,\n \n // \u4fbf\u6377\u65b9\u6cd5\n isCreating: create[Entity]Mutation.isPending,\n isUpdating: update[Entity]Mutation.isPending,\n isDeleting: delete[Entity]Mutation.isPending,\n isUpdatingStatus: updateStatus.isPending,\n }\n}\n```"}","mcp_server_name":"","mcp_tool_name":""}},{"id":0,"type":0,"content":"最后，生成基于Trevor Lasn博客的三层数据架构集成规则： 'react'\nimport { [Entity]WithRelations, [Entity]Filters } from '@/lib/repositories/[entity].repository'\n\ninterface [Entity]ContextValue {\n initial[Entities]: [Entity]WithRelations[]\n initialStats?: [Entity]Stats\n filters: [Entity]Filters\n loading: boolean\n error?: string\n}\n\nconst [Entity]Context = createContext<[Entity]ContextValue | undefined>(undefined)\n\ninterface [Entity]ProviderProps {\n children: ReactNode\n initial[Entities]: [Entity]WithRelations[]\n initialStats?: [Entity]Stats\n filters: [Entity]Filters\n loading?: boolean\n error?: string\n}\n\nexport function [Entity]Provider({\n children,\n initial[Entities],\n initialStats,\n filters,\n loading = false,\n error\n}: [Entity]ProviderProps) {\n const value: [Entity]ContextValue = {\n initial[Entities],\n initialStats,\n filters,\n loading,\n error\n }\n\n return (\n <[Entity]Context.Provider value={value}>\n {children}\n \n )\n}\n\nexport function use[Entity]Context() {\n const \n )\n}\n```\n\n## \u751f\u6210\u6307\u4ee4\u548c\u7ea6\u675f\n\n### \u4e09\u5c42\u67b6\u6784\u96c6\u6210\u539f\u5219\n1. **Layer 1 (Server Components)**: \u8d1f\u8d23\u521d\u59cb\u6570\u636e\u83b7\u53d6\uff0c\u63d0\u4f9bSEO\u53cb\u597d\u7684\u670d\u52a1\u7aef\u6e32\u67d3\n2. **Layer 2 (React Query)**: \u7ba1\u7406\u5ba2\u6237\u7aef\u72b6\u6001\uff0c\u63d0\u4f9b\u7f13\u5b58\u3001\u540c\u6b65\u548c\u9519\u8bef\u5904\u7406\n3. **Layer 3 (Optimistic Updates)**: \u63d0\u4f9b\u5373\u65f6UI\u53cd\u9988\uff0c\u589e\u5f3a\u7528\u6237\u4f53\u9a8c\n\n### \u6570\u636e\u6d41\u8bbe\u8ba1\u89c4\u8303\n- **\u521d\u59cb\u52a0\u8f7d**: Server Components \u2192 React Query\u521d\u59cb\u6570\u636e\n- **\u5ba2\u6237\u7aef\u66f4\u65b0**: React Query \u2192 Optimistic Updates \u2192 \u670d\u52a1\u7aef\u540c\u6b65\n- **\u9519\u8bef\u5904\u7406**: \u81ea\u52a8\u56de\u6eda\u4e50\u89c2\u66f4\u65b0\uff0c\u663e\u793a\u9519\u8bef\u4fe1\u606f\n- **\u7f13\u5b58\u7b56\u7565**: \u5408\u7406\u7684staleTime\u548cgcTime\u914d\u7f6e\n\n### \u6027\u80fd\u4f18\u5316\u8981\u6c42\n- **\u67e5\u8be2\u952e\u8bbe\u8ba1**: \u5c42\u6b21\u5316\u7684\u67e5\u8be2\u952e\u7ed3\u6784\uff0c\u652f\u6301\u7cbe\u786e\u7684\u7f13\u5b58\u5931\u6548\n- **\u4e50\u89c2\u66f4\u65b0**: \u5373\u65f6UI\u53cd\u9988\uff0c\u9519\u8bef\u65f6\u81ea\u52a8\u56de\u6eda\n- **\u5e76\u884c\u8bf7\u6c42**: \u4f7f\u7528Promise.all\u5e76\u884c\u83b7\u53d6\u76f8\u5173\u6570\u636e\n- **\u9aa8\u67b6\u5c4f**: \u63d0\u4f9b\u52a0\u8f7d\u72b6\u6001\u7684\u89c6\u89c9\u53cd\u9988\n\n## \u793a\u4f8b\u751f\u6210\u5bf9\u8bdd\n\n**\u7528\u6237**: \"\u8bf7\u4e3aTask\u5b9e\u4f53\u751f\u6210\u5b8c\u6574\u7684\u4e09\u5c42\u6570\u636e\u67b6\u6784\u96c6\u6210\u4ee3\u7801\"\n\n**\u4f60\u7684\u56de\u5e94\u6a21\u5f0f**:\n```\n\u6211\u5c06\u4e3aTask\u5b9e\u4f53\u751f\u6210\u5b8c\u6574\u7684\u4e09\u5c42\u6570\u636e\u67b6\u6784\u96c6\u6210\u4ee3\u7801\uff0c\u57fa\u4e8eTrevor Lasn\u7684\u67b6\u6784\u6a21\u5f0f\u3002\n\n\u67b6\u6784\u5c42\u6b21\uff1a\n1.
