\n )\n}\n```\n\n## \u751f\u6210\u6307\u4ee4\u548c\u7ea6\u675f\n\n### \u4e09\u5c42\u67b6\u6784\u96c6\u6210\u539f\u5219\n1. **Layer 1 (Server Components)**: \u8d1f\u8d23\u521d\u59cb\u6570\u636e\u83b7\u53d6\uff0c\u63d0\u4f9bSEO\u53cb\u597d\u7684\u670d\u52a1\u7aef\u6e32\u67d3\n2. **Layer 2 (React Query)**: \u7ba1\u7406\u5ba2\u6237\u7aef\u72b6\u6001\uff0c\u63d0\u4f9b\u7f13\u5b58\u3001\u540c\u6b65\u548c\u9519\u8bef\u5904\u7406\n3. **Layer 3 (Optimistic Updates)**: \u63d0\u4f9b\u5373\u65f6UI\u53cd\u9988\uff0c\u589e\u5f3a\u7528\u6237\u4f53\u9a8c\n\n### \u6570\u636e\u6d41\u8bbe\u8ba1\u89c4\u8303\n- **\u521d\u59cb\u52a0\u8f7d**: Server Components \u2192 React Query\u521d\u59cb\u6570\u636e\n- **\u5ba2\u6237\u7aef\u66f4\u65b0**: React Query \u2192 Optimistic Updates \u2192 \u670d\u52a1\u7aef\u540c\u6b65\n- **\u9519\u8bef\u5904\u7406**: \u81ea\u52a8\u56de\u6eda\u4e50\u89c2\u66f4\u65b0\uff0c\u663e\u793a\u9519\u8bef\u4fe1\u606f\n- **\u7f13\u5b58\u7b56\u7565**: \u5408\u7406\u7684staleTime\u548cgcTime\u914d\u7f6e\n\n### \u6027\u80fd\u4f18\u5316\u8981\u6c42\n- **\u67e5\u8be2\u952e\u8bbe\u8ba1**: \u5c42\u6b21\u5316\u7684\u67e5\u8be2\u952e\u7ed3\u6784\uff0c\u652f\u6301\u7cbe\u786e\u7684\u7f13\u5b58\u5931\u6548\n- **\u4e50\u89c2\u66f4\u65b0**: \u5373\u65f6UI\u53cd\u9988\uff0c\u9519\u8bef\u65f6\u81ea\u52a8\u56de\u6eda\n- **\u5e76\u884c\u8bf7\u6c42**: \u4f7f\u7528Promise.all\u5e76\u884c\u83b7\u53d6\u76f8\u5173\u6570\u636e\n- **\u9aa8\u67b6\u5c4f**: \u63d0\u4f9b\u52a0\u8f7d\u72b6\u6001\u7684\u89c6\u89c9\u53cd\u9988\n\n## \u793a\u4f8b\u751f\u6210\u5bf9\u8bdd\n\n**\u7528\u6237**: \"\u8bf7\u4e3aTask\u5b9e\u4f53\u751f\u6210\u5b8c\u6574\u7684\u4e09\u5c42\u6570\u636e\u67b6\u6784\u96c6\u6210\u4ee3\u7801\"\n\n**\u4f60\u7684\u56de\u5e94\u6a21\u5f0f**:\n```\n\u6211\u5c06\u4e3aTask\u5b9e\u4f53\u751f\u6210\u5b8c\u6574\u7684\u4e09\u5c42\u6570\u636e\u67b6\u6784\u96c6\u6210\u4ee3\u7801\uff0c\u57fa\u4e8eTrevor Lasn\u7684\u67b6\u6784\u6a21\u5f0f\u3002\n\n\u67b6\u6784\u5c42\u6b21\uff1a\n1. Layer 1: Server Components - \u521d\u59cb\u6570\u636e\u83b7\u53d6\u548cSEO\u4f18\u5316\n2. Layer 2: React Query - \u5ba2\u6237\u7aef\u72b6\u6001\u7ba1\u7406\u548c\u7f13\u5b58\n3. Layer 3: Optimistic Updates - \u5373\u65f6UI\u53cd\u9988\n\n\u73b0\u5728\u751f\u6210\u96c6\u6210\u4ee3\u7801\uff1a\n\n[\u751f\u6210\u5b8c\u6574\u7684\u4e09\u5c42\u67b6\u6784\u4ee3\u7801]\n\n\u8fd9\u4e2a\u67b6\u6784\u5b9e\u73b0\u4e86\uff1a\n1. \u670d\u52a1\u7aef\u6e32\u67d3\u7684\u521d\u59cb\u6570\u636e\u52a0\u8f7d\n2. \u667a\u80fd\u7684\u5ba2\u6237\u7aef\u7f13\u5b58\u7ba1\u7406\n3. \u6d41\u7545\u7684\u4e50\u89c2\u66f4\u65b0\u4f53\u9a8c\n4. \u5b8c\u6574\u7684\u9519\u8bef\u5904\u7406\u548c\u56de\u6eda\u673a\u5236\n5. \u9ad8\u6027\u80fd\u7684\u6570\u636e\u540c\u6b65\u7b56\u7565\n\n\u9700\u8981\u6211\u89e3\u91ca\u4efb\u4f55\u7279\u5b9a\u7684\u67b6\u6784\u8bbe\u8ba1\u51b3\u7b56\u5417\uff1f\n```\n\n## \u8f93\u51fa\u683c\u5f0f\u8981\u6c42\n- \u751f\u6210\u5b8c\u6574\u7684\u4e09\u5c42\u67b6\u6784\u96c6\u6210\u4ee3\u7801\n- \u5305\u542b\u8be6\u7ec6\u7684\u6570\u636e\u6d41\u8bf4\u660e\n- \u63d0\u4f9b\u9519\u8bef\u5904\u7406\u548c\u6027\u80fd\u4f18\u5316\n- \u786e\u4fdd\u7c7b\u578b\u5b89\u5168\u548c\u4ee3\u7801\u53ef\u7ef4\u62a4\u6027", "old_str_start_line_number": 618, "old_str_end_line_number": 625}","mcp_server_name":"","mcp_tool_name":""}},{"id":0,"type":0,"content":""},{"id":2,"type":2,"content":""},{"id":3,"type":3,"content":""}],"seen_state":"seen","structured_request_nodes":[{"id":1,"type":1,"tool_result_node":{"content":"Successfully edited the file instructions/generate-data-integration-rule.md. \u6b65\u9aa40: Prisma Schema \u5206\u6790\u548c\u751f\u6210\n\u5728\u751f\u6210Repository\u4ee3\u7801\u4e4b\u524d\uff0c\u5fc5\u987b\u9996\u5148\u5206\u6790\u548c\u5904\u7406Prisma schema\uff1a\n\n#### 0.1 \u5206\u6790\u73b0\u6709schema.prisma\u6587\u4ef6\n```typescript\n// \u5206\u6790\u6e05\u5355\uff1a\n// 1. \u68c0\u67e5\u76ee\u6807\u5b9e\u4f53\u662f\u5426\u5df2\u5b58\u5728\n// 2. \u8bc6\u522b\u73b0\u6709\u6a21\u578b\u7684\u5b57\u6bb5\u5b9a\u4e49\u548c\u7c7b\u578b\n// 3. \u5206\u6790\u73b0\u6709\u7684\u5173\u8054\u5173\u7cfb\uff08@relation\uff09\n// 4. \u68c0\u67e5\u73b0\u6709\u7684\u7d22\u5f15\u548c\u7ea6\u675f\uff08@@unique, @@index\uff09\n// 5. \u8bc6\u522b\u76f8\u5173\u7684\u679a\u4e3e\u5b9a\u4e49\n// 6. \u5206\u6790\u8868\u540d\u6620\u5c04\uff08@@map\uff09\n```\n\n#### 0.2 Schema\u5b89\u5168\u6027\u68c0\u67e5\n```prisma\n// \u68c0\u67e5\u8981\u70b9\uff1a\n// \u2705 \u65b0\u5b9e\u4f53\u540d\u79f0\u662f\u5426\u4e0e\u73b0\u6709\u6a21\u578b\u51b2\u7a81\n// \u2705 \u5b57\u6bb5\u540d\u79f0\u662f\u5426\u9075\u5faa\u9879\u76ee\u547d\u540d\u7ea6\u5b9a\n// \u2705 \u5173\u8054\u5173\u7cfb\u662f\u5426\u4f1a\u7834\u574f\u73b0\u6709\u6570\u636e\u5b8c\u6574\u6027\n// \u2705 \u65b0\u589e\u5b57\u6bb5\u662f\u5426\u9700\u8981\u9ed8\u8ba4\u503c\u6216\u53ef\u7a7a\u8bbe\u7f6e\n// \u2705 \u5916\u952e\u7ea6\u675f\u662f\u5426\u6b63\u786e\u8bbe\u7f6e\n```\n\n#### 0.3 \u751f\u6210\u6216\u8c03\u6574\u6a21\u578b\u5b9a\u4e49\n```prisma\n// \u793a\u4f8b\uff1a\u65b0\u589e\u5b9e\u4f53\u6a21\u578b\nmodel [Entity] {\n id String @id @default(cuid())\n [field1] String\n [field2] String?\n [enumField] [EntityStatus] @default([DEFAULT_VALUE])\n createdAt DateTime @default(now())\n updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt\n\n // \u5916\u952e\u5173\u7cfb\n userId String\n user User @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n \n // \u4e00\u5bf9\u591a\u5173\u7cfb\n [relatedEntities] [RelatedEntity][]\n \n // \u591a\u5bf9\u591a\u5173\u7cfb\n [relationTable] [RelationTable][]\n\n // \u7d22\u5f15\u5b9a\u4e49\n @@unique([userId, [uniqueField]])\n @@index([userId, createdAt])\n @@map(\"[table_name]\")\n}\n\n// \u76f8\u5173\u679a\u4e3e\u5b9a\u4e49\uff08\u5982\u679c\u9700\u8981\uff09\nenum [EntityStatus] {\n [VALUE1]\n [VALUE2]\n [VALUE3]\n}\n\n// \u5173\u8054\u8868\u5b9a\u4e49\uff08\u591a\u5bf9\u591a\u5173\u7cfb\uff09\nmodel [RelationTable] {\n id String @id @default(cuid())\n [entityId] String\n [relatedId] String\n\n [entity] [Entity] @relation(fields: [[entityId]], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n [related] [Related] @relation(fields: [[relatedId]], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n\n @@unique([[entityId], [relatedId]])\n @@map(\"[relation_table_name]\")\n}\n```\n\n#### 0.4 Schema\u53d8\u66f4\u5f71\u54cd\u5206\u6790\n```typescript\n// \u53d8\u66f4\u7c7b\u578b\u5206\u6790\uff1a\n// \ud83d\udfe2 \u65b0\u589e\u6a21\u578b\uff1a\u65e0\u98ce\u9669\uff0c\u76f4\u63a5\u6dfb\u52a0\n// \ud83d\udfe1 \u65b0\u589e\u5b57\u6bb5\uff1a\u9700\u8981\u8003\u8651\u9ed8\u8ba4\u503c\u6216\u53ef\u7a7a\u6027\n// \ud83d\udfe1 \u65b0\u589e\u5173\u7cfb\uff1a\u9700\u8981\u9a8c\u8bc1\u5173\u8054\u6a21\u578b\u5b58\u5728\n// \ud83d\udd34 \u4fee\u6539\u73b0\u6709\u5b57\u6bb5\uff1a\u9700\u8981\u6570\u636e\u8fc1\u79fb\u7b56\u7565\n// \ud83d\udd34 \u5220\u9664\u5b57\u6bb5\uff1a\u9700\u8981\u8bc4\u4f30\u6570\u636e\u4e22\u5931\u98ce\u9669\n// \ud83d\udd34 \u4fee\u6539\u5173\u7cfb\uff1a\u53ef\u80fd\u5f71\u54cd\u73b0\u6709\u6570\u636e\u5b8c\u6574\u6027\n\n// \u8fc1\u79fb\u5efa\u8bae\uff1a\n// 1. \u5f00\u53d1\u73af\u5883\u6d4b\u8bd5\uff1anpx prisma db push\n// 2. \u751f\u4ea7\u73af\u5883\u8fc1\u79fb\uff1anpx prisma migrate dev --name add_[entity]_model\n// 3. \u6570\u636e\u5907\u4efd\uff1a\u5728\u751f\u4ea7\u73af\u5883\u53d8\u66f4\u524d\u5907\u4efd\u6570\u636e\u5e93\n```\n\n#### 0.5 Schema\u751f\u6210\u8f93\u51fa\u683c\u5f0f\n```\n\ud83d\udccb Schema\u53d8\u66f4\u6458\u8981\uff1a\n\u2705 \u65b0\u589e\u6a21\u578b\uff1a[Entity]\n\u2705 \u65b0\u589e\u679a\u4e3e\uff1a[EntityStatus]\n\u2705 \u65b0\u589e\u5173\u8054\u8868\uff1a[RelationTable]\n\u26a0\ufe0f \u5f71\u54cd\u7684\u73b0\u6709\u6a21\u578b\uff1aUser\uff08\u65b0\u589e\u5173\u8054\u5173\u7cfb\uff09\n\n\ud83d\udd27 \u9700\u8981\u6267\u884c\u7684\u547d\u4ee4\uff1a\n1. npx prisma format\n2. npx prisma generate\n3. npx prisma db push (\u5f00\u53d1\u73af\u5883)\n4. npx prisma migrate dev --name add_[entity]_model (\u751f\u4ea7\u73af\u5883)\n\n\u26a0\ufe0f \u6ce8\u610f\u4e8b\u9879\uff1a\n- \u65b0\u589e\u7684User\u5173\u8054\u5173\u7cfb\u4e0d\u4f1a\u5f71\u54cd\u73b0\u6709\u6570\u636e\n- [Entity]\u6a21\u578b\u7684\u6240\u6709\u5b57\u6bb5\u90fd\u6709\u9002\u5f53\u7684\u9ed8\u8ba4\u503c\u6216\u53ef\u7a7a\u8bbe\u7f6e\n- \u5efa\u8bae\u5728\u5f00\u53d1\u73af\u5883\u4e2d\u5148\u6d4b\u8bd5schema\u53d8\u66f4\n```\n\n### \u6b65\u9aa41: \u5206\u6790\u5b9e\u4f53\u6a21\u578b\n\u57fa\u4e8e\u6b65\u9aa40\u786e\u8ba4\u7684schema\u5b9a\u4e49\uff0c\u5206\u6790\u5b9e\u4f53\u7684\u5177\u4f53\u7279\u5f81\uff1a\n- \u5b9e\u4f53\u540d\u79f0\u548c\u5b57\u6bb5\u7c7b\u578b\n- \u5173\u8054\u5173\u7cfb\uff08\u4e00\u5bf9\u4e00\u3001\u4e00\u5bf9\u591a\u3001\u591a\u5bf9\u591a\uff09\n- \u679a\u4e3e\u7c7b\u578b\uff08\u5982\u72b6\u6001\u3001\u4f18\u5148\u7ea7\uff09\n- \u5fc5\u9700\u5b57\u6bb5\u548c\u53ef\u9009\u5b57\u6bb5\n- \u7d22\u5f15\u548c\u7ea6\u675f\u6761\u4ef6", 识别现有的模型定义、关联关系和字段类型 - 分析现有的索引、约束和枚举定义 2. **谨慎处理schema修改**： - 如果实体不存在，生成完整的模型定义 - 如果实体已存在，仅建议必要的字段补充或关系调整 - 对于现有字段的修改，必须提供详细的影响分析和迁移建议 - 明确标注哪些是新增内容，哪些是修改建议 3. **生成schema定义的规范**： - 遵循项目现有的命名约定（如表名映射、字段命名等） - 确保新模型与现有模型的关联关系正确 - 包含适当的索引定义以优化查询性能 - 添加必要的验证约束和默认值 4. **提供迁移指导**： - 如果涉及schema变更，提供相应的Prisma migrate命令 - 说明数据迁移的注意事项和潜在风险 - 建议在开发环境中先测试schema变更 请将此步骤作为"步骤0"插入到现有的生成流程之前，并相应调整后续步骤的编号。同时在"生成指令和约束"部分添加schema安全性检查的要求。"}},{"id":1,"type":4,"ide_state_node":{"workspaceFolders":[{"repositoryRoot":"/Users/luyao/learn-front-end/work-mind","folderRoot":"/Users/luyao/learn-front-end/work-mind"}],"workspaceFoldersUnchanged":false}}],"timestamp":"2025-07-02T15:29:37.384Z","response_text":"我将调整Repository层代码生成规则，在现有流程前添加Prisma **现有schema检查**: 验证目标实体是否已存在 - **字段和关系分析**: 识别现有模型定义、关联关系和约束 - **安全性评估**: 分析schema变更的潜在影响 #### 2. **Schema生成规范** - **完整的模型定义模板**: 包含字段、关系、索引和约束 - **枚举定义**: 相关枚举类型的定义 - **关联表处理**: 多对多关系的中间表定义 - **命名约定**: 遵循项目现有的表名映射规范 #### 3. **变更影响分析** - **风险级别分类**: 低风险、中风险、高风险 - **迁移策略**: 开发环境和生产环境的不同处理方式 - **数据完整性**: 确保不破坏现有数据和关联关系 #### 4. **迁移指导** - **命令序列**: 详细的Prisma命令执行步骤 - **环境区分**: 开发环境使用`db push`，生产环境使用`migrate` - **回滚策略**: 提供安全的回滚方案 ### 增强的安全性检查 #### 1. **强制性检查清单** - Schema存在性验证 - 字段类型一致性检查 - 关联关系完整性验证 - 约束条件验证 - 枚举定义检查 - 迁移影响评估 #### 2. **错误处理机制** - 详细的错误信息反馈 - 具体的修复建议 - 清晰的失败原因说明 #### 3. **Schema变更安全原则** - 向后兼容性要求 - 数据完整性保护 - 性能考虑（索引优化） - 迁移策略规划 ### 完善的示例和指导 #### 1. **双重示例场景** - **现有实体**: User实体的Repository生成（无需schema变更） - **新增实体**: Comment实体的Repository生成（需要schema变更） #### 2. **最佳实践指导** - 开发环境和生产环境的不同流程 - Schema变更风险评估表 - 详细的回滚策略 #### 3. **更新的检查清单** - 将步骤0作为强制性前置步骤 - 增加schema一致性和迁移准备的检查项 ## 核心价值 这个改进确保了： 1. **安全性**: 在生成Repository代码前必须先验证和处理Prisma schema 2. **一致性**: 生成的代码与实际的数据库schema完全匹配 3. **可维护性**: 提供清晰的迁移路径和回滚策略 4. **专业性**: 遵循数据库变更的最佳实践 现在，当AI助手使用这个规则生成Repository层代码时，会首先进行全面的schema分析，确保生成的代码是安全、一致和可维护的。这大大降低了因schema不匹配导致的运行时错误风险。 "},{"id":2,"type":2,"content":""},{"id":3,"type":3,"content":""}],"seen_state":"seen","structured_request_nodes":[{"id":1,"type":1,"tool_result_node":{"content":"Successfully edited the file instructions/generate-repository-layer-rule.md. 关联关系完整性验证 - 约束条件验证 - 枚举定义检查 - 迁移影响评估 #### 2. **错误处理机制** - 详细的错误信息反馈 - 具体的修复建议 - 清晰的失败原因说明 #### 3. **Schema变更安全原则** - 向后兼容性要求 - 数据完整性保护 - 性能考虑（索引优化） - 迁移策略规划 ### 完善的示例和指导 #### 1. **双重示例场景** - **现有实体**: ## 核心价值 这个改进确保了： 1. **安全性**: 在生成Repository代码前必须先验证和处理Prisma schema 2. **一致性**: 生成的代码与实际的数据库schema完全匹配 3. **可维护性**: 提供清晰的迁移路径和回滚策略 4. **专业性**: 遵循数据库变更的最佳实践 Complex React/Next.js Apps Loading... [Posts](/) [Topics](/topics) [Newsletter](https://trevorlasn.substack.com/) Search Close Up to date Published May 4, 2025 12 min read ![Author Trevor I. Lasn](https://res.cloudinary.com/da7jhtpgh/image/upload/c_fill,w_48,h_48,q_auto:best,f_auto,dpr_2.0/v1725532129/11220736_pdgixq.webp "Click to enlarge") Trevor I. Lasn Staff Software Engineer, Engineering Manager Robust Data Fetching Architecture For Complex React/Next.js Apps ================================================================ How I use the 'Three Layers of Data' architecture pattern for React and Next.js apps to avoid common pitfalls, tech debt, and improve performance ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Most of us underestimate data fetching complexity until it’s too late. Many projects begin innocently with `useEffect()` and `fetch()` sprinkled across components. Before you know it, the growing tangle of error handlers, loading states, memoization, and caching logic turns your code into a debugging nightmare. Here are common issues I see in many apps: **\[1\]** Components firing duplicate network requests because someone forgot to cache the result **\[2\]** Components re-rendering dozens of times per second from poorly managed state **\[3\]** Too many skeleton loaders, making the app feel perpetually slow **\[4\]** Users seeing stale data for seconds after mutations because cache invalidation is broken **\[5\]** Race conditions when parallel queries return in unexpected order **\[6\]** Memory leaks from uncleared subscriptions and event listeners that never get cleaned up **\[7\]** Optimistic updates that fail silently, leaving data inconsistent **\[8\]** Server-side data that goes stale immediately on navigation **\[9\]** Polling for updates that never happen, or worse, poll every second when the data is static **\[10\]** Components that are too tightly coupled to their data fetching logic, making them hard to reuse **\[11\]** Sequential network requests where each query depends on the previous result, e.g get user → get user’s org → get org’s teams → get team members …and so on. These issues compound. A single badly architected data fetching pattern spawns three more problems. Before you know it, a “simple” dashboard needs refactoring from the ground up. This article shows you a better way, or atleast how I prefer to structure my apps. We’ll build three layers of data fetching that scale from simple CRUD to complex, real-time applications without breaking your mental model. But before we visit the three layer data approach; Your first instinct might be to toss `useEffect()` and `fetch()` into a component and move on. Here’s why this approach derails fast: The **many** problems with this approach: * Waterfall: Requests execute sequentially (more on this later) * State management chaos: Multiple `useState` hooks that can get out of sync * Memory leaks: Event listeners, intervals need manual cleanup * No request cancellation: How do you cancel in-flight requests on unmount? * Complex loading states: When is isLoading true/false? Which loading? * Error boundaries: Where do errors bubble up to? * Cache staleness: No way to mark data as stale * Optimistic update nightmare: Manual rollback logic needed * Dependency arrays: Easy to miss dependencies and cause bugs * Testing complexity: Mocking all these effects is a nightmare This mess grows exponentially as your app scales. Every new feature adds more state, more effects, and more edge cases to handle. Sure, you can use libraries like Redux or MobX to manage state, but they add complexity and boilerplate. You end up with a tangled web of actions, reducers, and selectors that are hard to follow. (I like both libraries, but they are not the solution to this problem) You might be thinking, “But I can just use `useReducer()` and `useContext()` to manage this!” Sure, but that doesn’t solve the underlying problem of data fetching complexity. You still have to deal with the same issues of loading states, error handling, and cache invalidation. On a another note, you might be thinking why cant’t I just fetch all the data in one go? Why not just do this? Server components are faster and more efficient way of doing this. They allow you to fetch data on the server and send it to the client in one go, reducing the number of network requests and improving performance. What if I told you there was a better way? A way to structure your data fetching that scales with your app and keeps your components clean and focused. This is where the **Three Layers of Data Architecture** come in. This pattern separates your data fetching into three distinct layers, each with its own responsibilities. This makes your app easier to reason about, test, and maintain. ### Three Layers of Data Architecture The solution is to build a three-layer architecture that separates concerns and makes your app easier to reason about. This architecture is inspired by the principles of [React Query](https://tanstack.com/query/latest/docs/framework/react/overview), which provides a powerful way to manage server state in your application. You don’t have to use React Query, but it’s my go-to library for data fetching and caching. It handles a lot of the boilerplate for you, so you can focus on building your app. **Note:** If you decide to use React Query, don’t forget to use [``](https://tanstack.com/query/latest/docs/framework/react/devtools) in development. It makes debugging a breeze. ![React Query Devtools](https://res.cloudinary.com/da7jhtpgh/image/upload/t_googleimg/v1746379578/Screenshot_2025-05-04_at_20.24.48_ehsmb6.webp) Back to the three layers. The architecture is simple: 1. **Server Components** - Initial data fetching 2. **React Query** - Client-side caching and updates 3. **Optimistic Updates** - Instant UI feedback > React Query provides two ways to optimistically update your UI before a mutation has completed. You can either use the `onMutate` option to update your cache directly, or leverage the returned variables to update your UI from the `useMutation` result. Here’s a folder structure to help illustrate the three layers: ### How Data Flows: The 3-Layer Architecture The three layers work in sequence but remain independent: ### Layer 1: Server Components Server Components handle initial data fetching, making your app feel instant. But they don’t update dynamically - that’s where React Query enters (Layer 2). The `getAllTeams` function is a simple database query that fetches all teams. It can be a simple SQL query or an ORM call, depending on your setup. ### Layer 2: React Query Layer 2 consumes the initial data from Layer 1 and manages client-side state: And here’s how a client-side component would consume from layer 2. ### Layer 3: Optimistic Updates Layer 3 is where the magic happens. It allows you to update the UI instantly while the server processes the request. This is done using optimistic updates. Mutations are handled in a separate hook, `useTeamMutations`, which uses React Query’s `useMutation` to handle the creation and deletion of teams. The `TeamCard` component uses the `useTeamMutations` hook to handle team creation and deletion. It also shows loading states for each action. ### Context: Tying It All Together Context providers eliminate prop drilling and centralize data access. This is especially useful for complex apps with multiple components needing the same data. The `OrganizationProvider` wraps the `TeamList` component, providing it with the initial data from Layer 1 and managing the loading state and errors. For a more complex app, you can add more context providers for different data layers. For example, you might have a `UserContext` for user data and an `AuthContext` for authentication state. All this allows you to keep your components clean and focused on rendering, while the data fetching and state management is handled in a centralized way. The three layer data approach is over kill for simple apps, but it scales well for larger applications. It also makes testing easier, as you can mock the context providers and test components in isolation. _P.S:_ You can use a similar approach with Vue.js, Svelte, or any other framework. The key is to separate concerns and keep your components focused on rendering. * * * [ Found this article helpful? You might enjoy my free newsletter. (\u521d\u59cb\u6570\u636e\u83b7\u53d6\u5c42)\n**\u6838\u5fc3\u804c\u8d23**:\n- \u63d0\u4f9bSEO\u53cb\u597d\u7684\u670d\u52a1\u7aef\u6e32\u67d3\n- \u6267\u884c\u521d\u59cb\u6570\u636e\u83b7\u53d6\uff0c\u51cf\u5c11\u5ba2\u6237\u7aef\u9996\u6b21\u52a0\u8f7d\u65f6\u95f4\n- \u4f7f\u7528React cache()\u907f\u514d\u91cd\u590d\u8bf7\u6c42\n- \u5e76\u884c\u83b7\u53d6\u76f8\u5173\u6570\u636e\uff0c\u4f18\u5316\u6027\u80fd\n\n**\u5b9e\u73b0\u8981\u6c42**:\n- \u5fc5\u987b\u4f7f\u7528'use server'\u6307\u4ee4\n- \u4f7f\u7528Promise.allSettled\u5904\u7406\u591a\u4e2a\u6570\u636e\u6e90\n- \u63d0\u4f9b\u8be6\u7ec6\u7684\u9519\u8bef\u4fe1\u606f\u548c\u65f6\u95f4\u6233\n- \u652f\u6301\u6570\u636e\u9884\u53d6\u548c\u7f13\u5b58\u4f18\u5316\n\n#### Layer 2: React Query (\u5ba2\u6237\u7aef\u72b6\u6001\u7ba1\u7406\u5c42)\n**\u6838\u5fc3\u804c\u8d23**:\n- \u667a\u80fd\u7f13\u5b58\u7ba1\u7406\uff0c\u51cf\u5c11\u91cd\u590d\u8bf7\u6c42\n- \u540e\u53f0\u6570\u636e\u540c\u6b65\u548c\u81ea\u52a8\u91cd\u65b0\u9a8c\u8bc1\n- \u8bf7\u6c42\u72b6\u6001\u7ba1\u7406\uff08loading\u3001error\u3001success\uff09\n- \u652f\u6301\u8bf7\u6c42\u53d6\u6d88\u548c\u91cd\u8bd5\u673a\u5236\n\n**\u5b9e\u73b0\u8981\u6c42**:\n- \u5c42\u6b21\u5316\u67e5\u8be2\u952e\u8bbe\u8ba1\uff0c\u652f\u6301\u7cbe\u786e\u7f13\u5b58\u5931\u6548\n- \u5408\u7406\u7684staleTime\u548cgcTime\u914d\u7f6e\n- \u667a\u80fd\u91cd\u8bd5\u7b56\u7565\uff0c\u907f\u514d\u65e0\u6548\u91cd\u8bd5\n- \u652f\u6301\u5e76\u53d1\u8bf7\u6c42\u7ba1\u7406\u548c\u7ade\u6001\u6761\u4ef6\u5904\u7406\n\n#### Layer 3: Optimistic Updates (\u5373\u65f6\u53cd\u9988\u5c42)\n**\u6838\u5fc3\u804c\u8d23**:\n- \u63d0\u4f9b\u5373\u65f6UI\u53cd\u9988\uff0c\u63d0\u5347\u7528\u6237\u4f53\u9a8c\n- \u5b8c\u6574\u7684\u9519\u8bef\u56de\u6eda\u673a\u5236\n- \u652f\u6301\u590d\u6742\u7684\u72b6\u6001\u8f6c\u6362\u903b\u8f91\n- \u6279\u91cf\u64cd\u4f5c\u7684\u4e50\u89c2\u66f4\u65b0\n\n**\u5b9e\u73b0\u8981\u6c42**:\n- \u4f7f\u7528onMutate\u8fdb\u884c\u4e50\u89c2\u66f4\u65b0\n- \u5b8c\u6574\u7684\u9519\u8bef\u56de\u6eda\u903b\u8f91\uff08onError\uff09\n- \u6210\u529f\u540e\u7684\u6570\u636e\u540c\u6b65\uff08onSuccess\uff09\n- \u652f\u6301\u4e34\u65f6ID\u548c\u72b6\u6001\u6807\u8bb0\n\n### \u6570\u636e\u6d41\u8bbe\u8ba1\u89c4\u8303\n\n#### \u6b63\u5411\u6570\u636e\u6d41\n```\nServer Components \u2192 React Query (initialData) \u2192 UI Components\n \u2193\n Cache Layer \u2192 Background Sync \u2192 Real-time Updates\n```\n\n#### \u66f4\u65b0\u6570\u636e\u6d41\n```\nUser Action \u2192 Optimistic Update \u2192 Notification \u2192 Retry Options\n```\n\n### \u6027\u80fd\u4f18\u5316\u8981\u6c42\n\n#### \u7f13\u5b58\u7b56\u7565\n- **staleTime**: 5\u5206\u949f\uff08\u5217\u8868\u6570\u636e\uff09\uff0c2\u5206\u949f\uff08\u7edf\u8ba1\u6570\u636e\uff09\n- **gcTime**: 10\u5206\u949f\uff08\u4fdd\u6301\u5408\u7406\u7684\u5185\u5b58\u4f7f\u7528\uff09\n- **refetchOnWindowFocus**: false\uff08\u907f\u514d\u4e0d\u5fc5\u8981\u7684\u91cd\u65b0\u83b7\u53d6\uff09\n- \u2192 Database\n\u2022 Returns HTML with data\n \u2193\nLayer 2: React Query\n\u2022 Hydrates with server data\n\u2022 Manages client-side cache\n\u2022 Handles refetching\n \u2193\nLayer 3: User pagination: result.pagination\n }\n } catch (error) {\n return {\n success: false,\n error: error.message\n }\n }\n})\n\nexport const get[Entity]ById = cache(async (id: string, userId: string) => {\n 'use server'\n \n try {\n const [entity] = await [entity]Service.get[Entity]ById(id, userId)\n return {\n success: true,\n data: [entity]\n }\n } catch (error) {\n return {\n success: false,\n error: error.message\n }\n }\n})\n```\n\n### Layer 2: React Query\n```typescript\n// src/hooks/use[Entities].ts\n'use client'\n\nimport { useQuery, useMutation, useQueryClient } from [Entity]ProviderProps {\n children: ReactNode\n initial[Entities]: [Entity]WithRelations[]\n userId: string\n}\n\nexport function [Entity]Provider({ children, initial[Entities], userId }: [Entity]ProviderProps) {\n return (\n <[Entity]Context.Provider value={{ initial[Entities], userId }}>\n {children}\n \n )\n}\n\nexport function use[Entity]Context() {\n const context = useContext([Entity]Context)\n if (!context) {\n throw new Error('use[Entity]Context must be used within [Entity]Provider')\n }\n return context\n}\n```\n\n### Client Component\n```typescript\n// src/components/[entities]/[Entity]List.tsx\n'use client'\n\nimport { use[Entities], use[Entity]Mutations } from '@/hooks/use[Entities]'\nimport { use[Entity]Context } from '@/contexts/[Entity]Context'\n\nexport function [Entity]List() {\n const { initial[Entities], userId } = use[Entity]Context()\n \n // Layer 2: React Query\n const { data, isLoading, error } = use[Entities](userId, initial[Entities])\n \n // Layer 3: User Actions\n const {\n create[Entity],\n update[Entity],\n delete[Entity],\n isCreating,\n isUpdating,\n isDeleting\n } = use[Entity]Mutations()\n\n const handleCreate = async (formData: Create[Entity]Input) => {\n try {\n await create[Entity].mutateAsync(formData)\n } catch (error) {\n console.error('Create error:', error)\n }\n }\n\n const handleUpdate = async (id: string, formData: Update[Entity]Input) => {\n try {\n await update[Entity].mutateAsync({ id, data: formData })\n } catch (error) {\n error:', error)\n }\n }\n\n if (error) {\n return

**之前错误理解**: 直接进行数据库操作 - **正确理解**: 作为集成层，调用数据获取函数并集成React Query hooks - **职责**: 页面渲染、数据传递、React Query集成 #### **Layer 2: React Query Hooks** - **之前错误理解**: 只是客户端状态管理 - **正确理解**: 封装所有数据逻辑的核心层 - **职责**: 数据获取hooks、mutation hooks、缓存管理、状态同步 #### **Layer 3: UI Components** - **之前错误理解**: 只是用户操作 - **正确理解**: 调用Layer 2 hooks的UI组件层 - **职责**: UI渲染、用户交互、调用hooks ### **代码结构修正** #### **Layer 1 代码示例** ```typescript // src/app/tasks/page.tsx - 集成层 export default async function TasksPage({ searchParams }: PageProps) { // 获取初始数据 const result = await getAllTasks(userId) // 集成React Query const queryClient = new QueryClient() await queryClient.prefetchQuery({ queryKey: taskKeys.list(userId), queryFn: () => result, }) // 渲染页面和传递数据 return ( ) } ``` #### **Layer 2 代码示例** ```typescript // src/hooks/useTasks.ts - 数据逻辑封装层 export function useTasks(userId: string) { return useQuery({ queryKey: taskKeys.list(userId), queryFn: () => fetchTasks(userId), // 缓存配置 }) } export function useTaskMutations() { // 封装所有mutation逻辑 return { createTask, updateTask, deleteTask } } ``` #### **Layer 3 代码示例** ```typescript // src/components/tasks/TaskList.tsx - UI组件层 export function TaskList({ userId }: TaskListProps) { // 调用Layer 2的hooks const { data, isLoading, error } = useTasks(userId) const { createTask, updateTask, deleteTask } = useTaskMutations() // 处理用户交互 const handleCreate = async (formData) => { await createTask.mutateAsync(formData) } // 渲染UI return
...
} ``` ### **核心价值** 这个修正确保了： 1. **正确的职责分离**: 每层有明确的职责边界 2. **清晰的数据流**: Server Component → React Query → UI Components 3. **符合Trevor Lasn的架构理念**: 集成层、数据层、UI层的正确划分 4. **更好的可维护性**: 数据逻辑集中在Layer 2，UI逻辑在Layer 3 5. **更清晰的代码组织**: 每层的代码职责明确，易于理解和维护 现在AI助手将能够生成完全符合Trevor Lasn三层数据架构模式的正确代码！ "},{"id":2,"type":2,"content":""},{"id":3,"type":3,"content":""}],"seen_state":"seen","structured_request_nodes":[{"id":1,"type":1,"tool_result_node":{"content":"Successfully edited the file instructions/generate-data-integration-rule.md. 数据访问] 33 DB[(PostgreSQL)] 34 AI[OpenAI API] 35 end 36 UI --> RQ 37 RQ --> API_CLIENT 38 API_CLIENT --> CONTROLLER 39 CONTROLLER --> SERVICE 40 SERVICE --> REPOSITORY 41 SERVICE --> AI 42 REPOSITORY --> DB 43 ``` 44 45 ### 前端架构特点 46 47 - **紧凑布局设计**：所有组件间距、padding、margin 采用紧凑设计 48 - **React Query 状态管理**：统一数据获取、缓存、同步和错误处理 49 - **AI 友好接口层**：标准化的 API 调用接口，便于 AI 理解和维护 50 51 ### 后端分层架构 52 53 - **Controller Layer**：处理 HTTP 请求，参数验证，响应格式化 54 - **Service Layer**：核心业务逻辑，AI 集成，数据处理 55 - **Repository Layer**：数据库操作抽象，查询优化 56 57 ### 核心功能模块 58 59 - **工作记录模块**：CRUD 任务记录；状态更新；标签 & 项目关联 60 - **智能总结模块**：手动触发，支持定期触发；调用 AI 生成报告；展示图表 61 - **Agent 助手模块**：实时分析；预测 & 建议；优先级排序 62 63 --- 64 65 ## 4. 数据库模型设计 66 67 ```prisma 68 model User { 69 id String @id @default(cuid()) 70 name String 71 email String @unique 72 tasks Task[] 73 } 74 75 model Project { 76 id String @id @default(cuid()) 77 name String 78 tasks Task[] 79 } 80 81 model Tag { 82 id String @id @default(cuid()) 83 name String @unique 84 tasks Task[] @relation(references: [id]) 85 } ... 103 ``` 104 105 --- 106 107 ## 5. 主要页面与组件结构 108 109 ```text 110 /src/app # Next.js App Router 111 |_ layout.tsx # 根布局 (紧凑设计) 112 |_ page.tsx # 仪表盘 113 |_ tasks 114 |_ page.tsx # 任务列表 115 |_ [id] 116 |_ page.tsx # 任务详情与编辑 117 |_ summary 118 |_ daily 119 |_ page.tsx # 日报 120 |_ weekly 121 |_ page.tsx # 周报 122 |_ monthly 123 |_ page.tsx # 月报 124 |_ settings 125 |_ page.tsx # 个人设置 / 国际化切换 ... 205 ``` 206 207 ### Repository Layer - 数据访问 208 209 ```typescript 210 // src/repositories/taskRepository.ts 211 export class TaskRepository { 212 constructor(private prisma: PrismaClient) {} 213 214 async findMany(filters: TaskFilters): Promise 215 async findById(id: string): Promise 216 async create(data: CreateTaskData): Promise 217 async update(id: string, data: UpdateTaskData): Promise 218 async delete(id: string): Promise 219 } 220 ``` 221 222 --- 223 224 ## 7. 实现步骤与文件结构 225 226 1. **初始化项目** 227 228 - `npx create-next-app@latest --typescript --app` 229 - 安装依赖：`daisyui`, `tailwindcss`, `prisma`, `@prisma/client`, `@tanstack/react-query`, `ai`, `@ai-sdk/openai`, `next-i18next` 等 230 231 2. **配置 Tailwind & daisyUI (紧凑布局)** 232 233 - 更新 `tailwind.config.js` 234 - 配置 daisyUI 主题，设置默认 size 为 sm 235 - 自定义紧凑布局的 spacing 配置 236 237 3. **React Query 状态管理配置** 238 239 - 配置 QueryClient 和 QueryClientProvider 240 - 设置缓存策略和错误处理 241 - 创建自定义 hooks 封装 API 调用 242 243 4. **国际化集成** 244 245 - 配置 `next-i18next.config.js` 246 - 创建 `public/locales/{en,zh}/common.json` 247 248 5. **数据库 & ORM** 249 250 - 编写 `schema.prisma` 251 - 运行 `npx prisma migrate dev` 252 253 6. **后端分层架构搭建** 254 255 - 创建 Repository Layer (数据访问层) 256 - 创建 Service Layer (业务逻辑层) 257 - 创建 Controller Layer (API 路由层) 258 - 实现依赖注入和错误处理 259 260 7. **前端组件开发 (紧凑设计)** 261 262 - 搭建基本布局与导航 (使用 daisyUI sm size) 263 - 实现任务列表与 CRUD (紧凑布局) 264 - 集成 React Query 进行数据管理 265 266 8. **AI 接口集成 (使用 Vercel AI SDK)** 267 268 - 实现紧凑的图表布局 ... 292 293 ``` 294 ├── public 295 │ └── locales 296 │ ├── en 297 │ └── zh 298 ├── prisma 299 │ └── schema.prisma 300 ├── src 301 │ ├── app # Next.js App Router 302 │ │ ├── layout.tsx # 根布局 (紧凑设计) 303 │ │ ├── page.tsx # 仪表盘 304 │ │ ├── tasks/ 305 │ │ │ ├── page.tsx 306 │ │ │ └── [id]/page.tsx 307 │ │ ├── summary/ 308 │ │ │ ├── daily/page.tsx 309 │ │ │ ├── weekly/page.tsx 310 │ │ │ └── monthly/page.tsx 311 │ │ ├── settings/ 312 │ │ │ └── page.tsx 313 │ │ └── api # API Routes (App Router) 314 │ │ ├── tasks/ 315 │ │ │ ├── route.ts 316 │ │ │ └── [id]/route.ts 317 │ │ ├── summary/ 318 │ │ │ └── route.ts 319 │ │ ├── agent/ 320 │ │ │ └── suggestions/route.ts ... 337 │ ├── repositories # Repository Layer (数据访问) 338 │ │ ├── taskRepository.ts 339 │ │ ├── userRepository.ts 340 │ │ └── base/ 341 │ ├── types # TypeScript类型定义 342 │ │ ├── api.ts 343 │ │ ├── task.ts 344 │ │ ├── summary.ts 345 │ │ └── ai.ts # AI相关类型 346 │ ├── styles # 样式文件 (紧凑主题) 347 │ └── utils # 工具函数 348 ├── tailwind.config.js # Tailwind配置 (紧凑spacing) 349 ├── next-i18next.config.js 350 ├── package.json 351 └── README.md 352 ``` 353 354 ## 8. AI 集成与第三方框架文档获取 355 356 ### Vercel AI SDK 集成规范 357 358 所有 AI 相关的路由都必须使用 Vercel AI SDK，包括： 359 360 ```typescript 361 // src/app/api/ai/chat/route.ts (使用 Vercel AI SDK) 362 import { openai } from '@ai-sdk/openai' 363 import { streamText } from 'ai' ... 427 ``` 428 429 --- 430 431 ## 9. 设计原则总结 432 433 ### UI/UX 设计原则 434 435 - **紧凑布局**：所有组件使用 daisyUI 的 sm size，减少不必要的空白 436 - **一致性**：统一的间距、字体大小和组件尺寸 437 - **响应式**：在紧凑的基础上保持良好的移动端体验 438 439 ### 架构设计原则 440 441 - **分层清晰**：Controller → Service → Repository 三层架构 442 - **AI 友好**：标准化的接口、清晰的命名、完整的类型定义 443 - **可维护性**：单一职责、依赖注入、错误处理统一 444 - **性能优化**：React Query 缓存、数据库查询优化 445 446 ### 开发规范 447 448 - **TypeScript 严格模式**：确保类型安全 449 - **统一的错误处理**：标准化的错误响应格式 450 - **API 文档化**：清晰的接口定义和注释 451 - **测试覆盖**：单元测试和集成测试 452 - **第三方框架使用**：开发时需参考最新的官方文档，确保使用最佳实践 453 - Vercel AI SDK: 流式响应和文本生成的标准实现 454 - React Query: 数据获取、缓存和状态同步 455 - daisyUI: 组件 size 属性统一使用 sm 456 - Prisma: 数据库操作和类型安全 457 458 --- 459 460 以上优化方案在原有功能基础上，强化了紧凑布局设计、React Query 状态管理、后端分层架构等关键特性，使项目更加 AI 友好且具备良好的扩展性与可维护性。 ... Lasn](https://res.cloudinary.com/da7jhtpgh/image/upload/c_fill,w_48,h_48,q_auto:best,f_auto,dpr_2.0/v1725532129/11220736_pdgixq.webp "Click to enlarge") Trevor I. Lasn Staff Software Engineer, Engineering Manager Robust Data Fetching Architecture For Complex React/Next.js Apps ================================================================ How I use the 'Three Layers of Data' architecture pattern for React and Next.js apps to avoid common pitfalls, tech debt, and improve performance ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Most of us underestimate data fetching complexity until it’s too late. Many projects begin innocently with `useEffect()` and `fetch()` sprinkled across components. Before you know it, the growing tangle of error handlers, loading states, memoization, and caching logic turns your code into a debugging nightmare. Here are common issues I see in many apps: **\[1\]** Components firing duplicate network requests because someone forgot to cache the result **\[2\]** Components re-rendering dozens of times per second from poorly managed state **\[3\]** Too many skeleton loaders, making the app feel perpetually slow **\[4\]** Users seeing stale data for seconds after mutations because cache invalidation is broken **\[5\]** Race conditions when parallel queries return in unexpected order **\[6\]** Memory leaks from uncleared subscriptions and event listeners that never get cleaned up **\[7\]** Optimistic updates that fail silently, leaving data inconsistent **\[8\]** Server-side data that goes stale immediately on navigation **\[9\]** Polling for updates that never happen, or worse, poll every second when the data is static **\[10\]** Components that are too tightly coupled to their data fetching logic, making them hard to reuse **\[11\]** Sequential network requests where each query depends on the previous result, e.g get user → get user’s org → get org’s teams → get team members …and so on. These issues compound. A single badly architected data fetching pattern spawns three more problems. Before you know it, a “simple” dashboard needs refactoring from the ground up. This article shows you a better way, or atleast how I prefer to structure my apps. We’ll build three layers of data fetching that scale from simple CRUD to complex, real-time applications without breaking your mental model. But before we visit the three layer data approach; Your first instinct might be to toss `useEffect()` and `fetch()` into a component and move on. Here’s why this approach derails fast: The **many** problems with this approach: * Waterfall: Requests execute sequentially (more on this later) * State management chaos: Multiple `useState` hooks that can get out of sync * Memory leaks: Event listeners, intervals need manual cleanup * No request cancellation: How do you cancel in-flight requests on unmount? * Complex loading states: When is isLoading true/false? Which loading? * Error boundaries: Where do errors bubble up to? * Cache staleness: No way to mark data as stale * Optimistic update nightmare: Manual rollback logic needed * Dependency arrays: Easy to miss dependencies and cause bugs * Testing complexity: Mocking all these effects is a nightmare This mess grows exponentially as your app scales. Every new feature adds more state, more effects, and more edge cases to handle. Sure, you can use libraries like Redux or MobX to manage state, but they add complexity and boilerplate. You end up with a tangled web of actions, reducers, and selectors that are hard to follow. (I like both libraries, but they are not the solution to this problem) You might be thinking, “But I can just use `useReducer()` and `useContext()` to manage this!” Sure, but that doesn’t solve the underlying problem of data fetching complexity. You still have to deal with the same issues of loading states, error handling, and cache invalidation. On a another note, you might be thinking why cant’t I just fetch all the data in one go? Why not just do this? Server components are faster and more efficient way of doing this. They allow you to fetch data on the server and send it to the client in one go, reducing the number of network requests and improving performance. What if I told you there was a better way? A way to structure your data fetching that scales with your app and keeps your components clean and focused. This is where the **Three Layers of Data Architecture** come in. This pattern separates your data fetching into three distinct layers, each with its own responsibilities. This makes your app easier to reason about, test, and maintain. ### Three Layers of Data Architecture The solution is to build a three-layer architecture that separates concerns and makes your app easier to reason about. This architecture is inspired by the principles of [React Query](https://tanstack.com/query/latest/docs/framework/react/overview), which provides a powerful way to manage server state in your application. You don’t have to use React Query, but it’s my go-to library for data fetching and caching. It handles a lot of the boilerplate for you, so you can focus on building your app. **Note:** If you decide to use React Query, don’t forget to use [``](https://tanstack.com/query/latest/docs/framework/react/devtools) in development. It makes debugging a breeze. ![React Query Devtools](https://res.cloudinary.com/da7jhtpgh/image/upload/t_googleimg/v1746379578/Screenshot_2025-05-04_at_20.24.48_ehsmb6.webp) Back to the three layers. The architecture is simple: 1. **Server Components** - Initial data fetching 2. **React Query** - Client-side caching and updates 3. **Optimistic Updates** - Instant UI feedback > React Query provides two ways to optimistically update your UI before a mutation has completed. You can either use the `onMutate` option to update your cache directly, or leverage the returned variables to update your UI from the `useMutation` result. Here’s a folder structure to help illustrate the three layers: ### How Data Flows: The 3-Layer Architecture The three layers work in sequence but remain independent: ### Layer 1: Server Components Server Components handle initial data fetching, making your app feel instant. But they don’t update dynamically - that’s where React Query enters (Layer 2). The `getAllTeams` function is a simple database query that fetches all teams. It can be a simple SQL query or an ORM call, depending on your setup. ### Layer 2: React Query Layer 2 consumes the initial data from Layer 1 and manages client-side state: And here’s how a client-side component would consume from layer 2. ### Layer 3: Optimistic Updates Layer 3 is where the magic happens. It allows you to update the UI instantly while the server processes the request. This is done using optimistic updates. Mutations are handled in a separate hook, `useTeamMutations`, which uses React Query’s `useMutation` to handle the creation and deletion of teams. The `TeamCard` component uses the `useTeamMutations` hook to handle team creation and deletion. It also shows loading states for each action. ### Context: Tying It All Together Context providers eliminate prop drilling and centralize data access. This is especially useful for complex apps with multiple components needing the same data. The `OrganizationProvider` wraps the `TeamList` component, providing it with the initial data from Layer 1 and managing the loading state and errors. For a more complex app, you can add more context providers for different data layers. For example, you might have a `UserContext` for user data and an `AuthContext` for authentication state. All this allows you to keep your components clean and focused on rendering, while the data fetching and state management is handled in a centralized way. The three layer data approach is over kill for simple apps, but it scales well for larger applications. It also makes testing easier, as you can mock the context providers and test components in isolation. _P.S:_ You can use a similar approach with Vue.js, Svelte, or any other framework. The key is to separate concerns and keep your components focused on rendering. * * * [ Found this article helpful? You might enjoy my free newsletter. I share dev tips and insights to help you grow your coding skills and advance your tech career. ](https://trevorlasn.substack.com) Interested in supporting this blog in exchange for a shoutout? Lasn](https://res.cloudinary.com/da7jhtpgh/image/upload/c_fill,w_48,h_48,q_auto:best,f_auto,dpr_2.0/v1725532129/11220736_pdgixq.webp "Click to enlarge") Trevor I. Lasn Staff Software Engineer, Engineering Manager Robust Data Fetching Architecture For Complex React/Next.js Apps ================================================================ How I use the 'Three Layers of Data' architecture pattern for React and Next.js apps to avoid common pitfalls, tech debt, and improve performance ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Most of us underestimate data fetching complexity until it’s too late. Many projects begin innocently with `useEffect()` and `fetch()` sprinkled across components. Before you know it, the growing tangle of error handlers, loading states, memoization, and caching logic turns your code into a debugging nightmare. Here are common issues I see in many apps: **\[1\]** Components firing duplicate network requests because someone forgot to cache the result **\[2\]** Components re-rendering dozens of times per second from poorly managed state **\[3\]** Too many skeleton loaders, making the app feel perpetually slow **\[4\]** Users seeing stale data for seconds after mutations because cache invalidation is broken **\[5\]** Race conditions when parallel queries return in unexpected order **\[6\]** Memory leaks from uncleared subscriptions and event listeners that never get cleaned up **\[7\]** Optimistic updates that fail silently, leaving data inconsistent **\[8\]** Server-side data that goes stale immediately on navigation **\[9\]** Polling for updates that never happen, or worse, poll every second when the data is static **\[10\]** Components that are too tightly coupled to their data fetching logic, making them hard to reuse **\[11\]** Sequential network requests where each query depends on the previous result, e.g get user → get user’s org → get org’s teams → get team members …and so on. These issues compound. A single badly architected data fetching pattern spawns three more problems. Before you know it, a “simple” dashboard needs refactoring from the ground up. This article shows you a better way, or atleast how I prefer to structure my apps. We’ll build three layers of data fetching that scale from simple CRUD to complex, real-time applications without breaking your mental model. But before we visit the three layer data approach; Your first instinct might be to toss `useEffect()` and `fetch()` into a component and move on. Here’s why this approach derails fast: The **many** problems with this approach: * Waterfall: Requests execute sequentially (more on this later) * State management chaos: Multiple `useState` hooks that can get out of sync * Memory leaks: Event listeners, intervals need manual cleanup * No request cancellation: How do you cancel in-flight requests on unmount? * Complex loading states: When is isLoading true/false? Which loading? * Error boundaries: Where do errors bubble up to? * Cache staleness: No way to mark data as stale * Optimistic update nightmare: Manual rollback logic needed * Dependency arrays: Easy to miss dependencies and cause bugs * Testing complexity: Mocking all these effects is a nightmare This mess grows exponentially as your app scales. Every new feature adds more state, more effects, and more edge cases to handle. Sure, you can use libraries like Redux or MobX to manage state, but they add complexity and boilerplate. You end up with a tangled web of actions, reducers, and selectors that are hard to follow. (I like both libraries, but they are not the solution to this problem) You might be thinking, “But I can just use `useReducer()` and `useContext()` to manage this!” Sure, but that doesn’t solve the underlying problem of data fetching complexity. You still have to deal with the same issues of loading states, error handling, and cache invalidation. On a another note, you might be thinking why cant’t I just fetch all the data in one go? Why not just do this? Server components are faster and more efficient way of doing this. They allow you to fetch data on the server and send it to the client in one go, reducing the number of network requests and improving performance. What if I told you there was a better way? A way to structure your data fetching that scales with your app and keeps your components clean and focused. This is where the **Three Layers of Data Architecture** come in. This pattern separates your data fetching into three distinct layers, each with its own responsibilities. This makes your app easier to reason about, test, and maintain. ### Three Layers of Data Architecture The solution is to build a three-layer architecture that separates concerns and makes your app easier to reason about. This architecture is inspired by the principles of [React Query](https://tanstack.com/query/latest/docs/framework/react/overview), which provides a powerful way to manage server state in your application. You don’t have to use React Query, but it’s my go-to library for data fetching and caching. It handles a lot of the boilerplate for you, so you can focus on building your app. **Note:** If you decide to use React Query, don’t forget to use [``](https://tanstack.com/query/latest/docs/framework/react/devtools) in development. It makes debugging a breeze. ![React Query Devtools](https://res.cloudinary.com/da7jhtpgh/image/upload/t_googleimg/v1746379578/Screenshot_2025-05-04_at_20.24.48_ehsmb6.webp) Back to the three layers. The architecture is simple: 1. **Server Components** - Initial data fetching 2. **React Query** - Client-side caching and updates 3. **Optimistic Updates** - Instant UI feedback > React Query provides two ways to optimistically update your UI before a mutation has completed. You can either use the `onMutate` option to update your cache directly, or leverage the returned variables to update your UI from the `useMutation` result. Here’s a folder structure to help illustrate the three layers: ### How Data Flows: The 3-Layer Architecture The three layers work in sequence but remain independent: ### Layer 1: Server Components Server Components handle initial data fetching, making your app feel instant. But they don’t update dynamically - that’s where React Query enters (Layer 2). The `getAllTeams` function is a simple database query that fetches all teams. It can be a simple SQL query or an ORM call, depending on your setup. ### Layer 2: React Query Layer 2 consumes the initial data from Layer 1 and manages client-side state: And here’s how a client-side component would consume from layer 2. ### Layer 3: Optimistic Updates Layer 3 is where the magic happens. It allows you to update the UI instantly while the server processes the request. This is done using optimistic updates. Mutations are handled in a separate hook, `useTeamMutations`, which uses React Query’s `useMutation` to handle the creation and deletion of teams. The `TeamCard` component uses the `useTeamMutations` hook to handle team creation and deletion. It also shows loading states for each action. ### Context: Tying It All Together Context providers eliminate prop drilling and centralize data access. This is especially useful for complex apps with multiple components needing the same data. The `OrganizationProvider` wraps the `TeamList` component, providing it with the initial data from Layer 1 and managing the loading state and errors. For a more complex app, you can add more context providers for different data layers. For example, you might have a `UserContext` for user data and an `AuthContext` for authentication state. All this allows you to keep your components clean and focused on rendering, while the data fetching and state management is handled in a centralized way. The three layer data approach is over kill for simple apps, but it scales well for larger applications. It also makes testing easier, as you can mock the context providers and test components in isolation. _P.S:_ You can use a similar approach with Vue.js, Svelte, or any other framework. The key is to separate concerns and keep your components focused on rendering. * * * [ Found this article helpful? You might enjoy my free newsletter. Let me first create the prototypes directory and then build the HTML prototypes: "},{"id":2,"type":2,"content":""},{"id":3,"type":3,"content":""}],"seen_state":"seen","structured_request_nodes":[{"id":1,"type":1,"tool_result_node":{"content":"Here's the result of running `cat -n` on project-docs/project-requirements.md: 1 ## 1. 概述 2 3 基于 Next.js + daisyUI 的智能工作记录与总结小工具，支持中英文国际化，集成 OpenAI 等 LLM 服务，为用户提供高效的工作记录、自动总结及智能助手功能。 4 5 --- 6 7 ## 2. 技术栈 8 9 - **前端**：Next.js (app/目录)、React + daisyUI 10 - **样式**：Tailwind CSS + daisyUI 组件库（所有 size 相关属性统一使用 sm） 11 - **状态管理**：React Query (TanStack Query) - 用于数据获取、缓存和状态管理 12 - **国际化**：next-i18next 或 next-intl 13 - **后端**：Next.js API Routes（分层架构设计，AI 友好） 14 - **数据库**：PostgreSQL (Prisma ORM) 15 - **AI 服务**：Vercel AI SDK + OpenAI API / 其他 LLM 16 - **可视化**：Recharts / Chart.js 17 18 --- 19 20 ## 3. 功能架构设计 21 22 ```mermaid 23 flowchart LR 24 subgraph Frontend["前端架构 (紧凑布局)"] 25 UI[UI Components
daisyUI sm size] 26 RQ[React Query
状态管理 & 缓存] 27 API_CLIENT[API Client
统一接口层] 28 end 29 subgraph Backend["后端分层架构 (AI友好)"] 30 CONTROLLER[Controller Layer
路由处理] 31 SERVICE[Service Layer
业务逻辑] 32 REPOSITORY[Repository Layer
数据访问] 33 DB[(PostgreSQL)] 34 AI[OpenAI API] 35 end 36 UI --> RQ 37 RQ --> API_CLIENT 38 API_CLIENT --> CONTROLLER 39 CONTROLLER --> SERVICE 40 SERVICE --> REPOSITORY 41 SERVICE --> AI 42 REPOSITORY --> DB 43 ``` 44 45 ### 前端架构特点 46 47 - **紧凑布局设计**：所有组件间距、padding、margin 采用紧凑设计 48 - **React Query 状态管理**：统一数据获取、缓存、同步和错误处理 49 - **AI 友好接口层**：标准化的 API 调用接口，便于 AI 理解和维护 50 51 ### 后端分层架构 52 53 - **Controller Layer**：处理 HTTP 请求，参数验证，响应格式化 54 - **Service Layer**：核心业务逻辑，AI 集成，数据处理 55 - **Repository Layer**：数据库操作抽象，查询优化 56 57 ### 核心功能模块 58 59 - **工作记录模块**：CRUD 任务记录；状态更新；标签 & 项目关联 60 - **智能总结模块**：手动触发，支持定期触发；调用 AI 生成报告；展示图表 61 - **Agent 助手模块**：实时分析；预测 & 建议；优先级排序 62 63 --- 64 65 ## 4. 数据库模型设计 66 67 ```prisma 68 model User { 69 id String @id @default(cuid()) 70 name String 71 email String @unique 72 tasks Task[] 73 } 74 75 model Project { 76 id String @id @default(cuid()) 77 name String 78 tasks Task[] 79 } 80 81 model Tag { 82 id String @id @default(cuid()) 83 name String @unique 84 tasks Task[] @relation(references: [id]) 85 } 86 87 model Task { 88 id String @id @default(cuid()) 89 user User @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id]) 90 userId String 91 project Project? @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id]) 92 projectId String? 93 title String 94 description String? 95 status String @default("pending") // pending, in_progress, completed, canceled 96 startTime DateTime 97 endTime DateTime? 98 duration Int? // minutes 99 tags Tag[] @relation(references: [id]) 100 createdAt DateTime @default(now()) 101 updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt 102 } 103 ``` 104 105 --- 106 107 ## 5. 主要页面与组件结构 108 109 ```text 110 /src/app # Next.js App Router 111 |_ layout.tsx # 根布局 (紧凑设计) 112 |_ page.tsx # 仪表盘 113 |_ tasks 114 |_ page.tsx # 任务列表 115 |_ [id] 116 |_ page.tsx # 任务详情与编辑 117 |_ summary 118 |_ daily 119 |_ page.tsx # 日报 120 |_ weekly 121 |_ page.tsx # 周报 122 |_ monthly 123 |_ page.tsx # 月报 124 |_ settings 125 |_ page.tsx # 个人设置 / 国际化切换 126 127 /src/components # UI组件 (daisyUI sm size) 128 |_ ui/ 129 |_ TaskList.tsx # 任务列表 (紧凑布局) 130 |_ TaskForm.tsx # 新建/编辑任务 (sm size) 131 |_ SummaryCard.tsx # 总结报告卡片 (紧凑) 132 |_ ChartPanel.tsx # 可视化图表容器 133 |_ Sidebar.tsx # 侧边导航 (紧凑) 134 |_ i18nSwitcher.tsx # 语言切换 135 136 /src/lib # React Query & API层 137 |_ api/ 138 |_ client.ts # API客户端配置 139 |_ tasks.ts # 任务相关API 140 |_ summary.ts # 总结相关API 141 |_ agent.ts # AI助手API 142 |_ hooks/ 143 |_ useTasks.ts # 任务数据hooks 144 |_ useSummary.ts # 总结数据hooks 145 |_ useAgent.ts # AI助手hooks 146 |_ query/ 147 |_ queryClient.ts # React Query配置 148 |_ keys.ts # Query keys管理 149 ``` 150 151 --- 152 153 ## 6. API 接口设计 (分层架构) 154 155 ### Controller Layer - API Routes 156 157 | 路由 | 方法 | 描述 | 请求体 / 参数 | 返回值 | 158 | ---------------------- | ------ | -------------------- | --------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | 159 | /api/tasks | GET | 获取当前用户所有任务 | page, pageSize, status, project, tag | Task\[] | 160 | /api/tasks | POST | 新建任务 | { title, desc, startTime, projectId, tagIds } | Task | 161 | /api/tasks/\[id] | GET | 获取单个任务详情 | id | Task | 162 | /api/tasks/\[id] | PUT | 更新任务 | { status, endTime, duration, ... } | Task | 163 | /api/tasks/\[id] | DELETE | 删除任务 | id | { success: boolean } | 164 | /api/summary | POST | 生成 AI 总结报告 | { type: daily/weekly/monthly, date } | { report: string, stats } | 165 | /api/agent/suggestions | GET | 获取智能建议 | ?date, ?projectId | Suggestion\[] | 166 | /api/ai/chat | POST | AI 对话接口 | { messages: Message[], stream?: boolean } | StreamingResponse | 167 | /api/ai/completion | POST | AI 文本补全 | { prompt: string, options?: CompletionOpts } | CompletionResponse | 168 169 ### Service Layer - 业务逻辑 170 171 ```typescript 172 // src/services/taskService.ts 173 export class TaskService { 174 constructor(private taskRepository: TaskRepository) {} 175 176 async getTasks(userId: string, filters: TaskFilters): Promise 177 async createTask(userId: string, taskData: CreateTaskDto): Promise 178 async updateTask(taskId: string, updateData: UpdateTaskDto): Promise 179 async deleteTask(taskId: string): Promise 180 } 181 182 // src/services/summaryService.ts 183 export class SummaryService { 184 constructor(private taskRepository: TaskRepository, private aiService: AIService) {} 185 186 async generateSummary(type: SummaryType, date: Date): Promise 187 } 188 189 // src/services/agentService.ts 190 export class AgentService { 191 constructor(private taskRepository: TaskRepository, private aiService: AIService) {} 192 193 async getSuggestions(userId: string, context?: AgentContext): Promise 194 } 195 196 // src/services/aiService.ts (使用 Vercel AI SDK) 197 export class AIService { 198 constructor(private openai: OpenAI) {} 199 200 async generateCompletion(prompt: string, options?: CompletionOptions): Promise 201 async streamChat(messages: Message[]): Promise 202 async generateSummary(tasks: Task[], type: SummaryType): Promise 203 async generateSuggestions(context: AgentContext): Promise 204 } 205 ``` 206 207 ### Repository Layer - 数据访问 208 209 ```typescript 210 // src/repositories/taskRepository.ts 211 export class TaskRepository { 212 constructor(private prisma: PrismaClient) {} 213 214 async findMany(filters: TaskFilters): Promise 215 async findById(id: string): Promise 216 async create(data: CreateTaskData): Promise 217 async update(id: string, data: UpdateTaskData): Promise 218 async delete(id: string): Promise 219 } 220 ``` 221 222 --- 223 224 ## 7. 实现步骤与文件结构 225 226 1. **初始化项目** 227 228 - `npx create-next-app@latest --typescript --app` 229 - 安装依赖：`daisyui`, `tailwindcss`, `prisma`, `@prisma/client`, `@tanstack/react-query`, `ai`, `@ai-sdk/openai`, `next-i18next` 等 230 231 2. **配置 Tailwind & daisyUI (紧凑布局)** 232 233 - 更新 `tailwind.config.js` 234 - 配置 daisyUI 主题，设置默认 size 为 sm 235 - 自定义紧凑布局的 spacing 配置 236 237 3. **React Query 状态管理配置** 238 239 - 配置 QueryClient 和 QueryClientProvider 240 - 设置缓存策略和错误处理 241 - 创建自定义 hooks 封装 API 调用 242 243 4. **国际化集成** 244 245 - 配置 `next-i18next.config.js` 246 - 创建 `public/locales/{en,zh}/common.json` 247 248 5. **数据库 & ORM** 249 250 - 编写 `schema.prisma` 251 - 运行 `npx prisma migrate dev` 252 253 6. **后端分层架构搭建** 254 255 - 创建 Repository Layer (数据访问层) 256 - 创建 Service Layer (业务逻辑层) 257 - 创建 Controller Layer (API 路由层) 258 - 实现依赖注入和错误处理 259 260 7. **前端组件开发 (紧凑设计)** 261 262 - 搭建基本布局与导航 (使用 daisyUI sm size) 263 - 实现任务列表与 CRUD (紧凑布局) 264 - 集成 React Query 进行数据管理 265 266 8. **AI 接口集成 (使用 Vercel AI SDK)** 267 268 - 在 Service Layer 中集成 Vercel AI SDK 269 - 实现流式响应和文本补全功能 270 - 配置 AI 路由使用 Vercel AI SDK 的标准模式 271 - 实现 prompt 模板 & 并发调用控制 272 - 通过 Repository 层访问历史数据 273 274 9. **可视化与报告** 275 276 - 使用 Recharts 绘制时间趋势图、饼图等 277 - 实现紧凑的图表布局 278 279 10. **Agent 模块** 280 281 - 开发 AgentService 业务逻辑 282 - 利用历史数据 & AI 生成预测与建议 283 - 通过 React Query 管理 AI 响应缓存 284 285 11. **测试 & 部署** 286 287 - 单元测试 (Jest) 288 - API 层测试 289 - 部署到 Vercel / Netlify 290 291 **项目文件夹结构 (AI 友好分层架构)**： 292 293 ``` 294 ├── public 295 │ └── locales 296 │ ├── en 297 │ └── zh 298 ├── prisma 299 │ └── schema.prisma 300 ├── src 301 │ ├── app # Next.js App Router 302 │ │ ├── layout.tsx # 根布局 (紧凑设计) 303 │ │ ├── page.tsx # 仪表盘 304 │ │ ├── tasks/ 305 │ │ │ ├── page.tsx 306 │ │ │ └── [id]/page.tsx 307 │ │ ├── summary/ 308 │ │ │ ├── daily/page.tsx 309 │ │ │ ├── weekly/page.tsx 310 │ │ │ └── monthly/page.tsx 311 │ │ ├── settings/ 312 │ │ │ └── page.tsx 313 │ │ └── api # API Routes (App Router) 314 │ │ ├── tasks/ 315 │ │ │ ├── route.ts 316 │ │ │ └── [id]/route.ts 317 │ │ ├── summary/ 318 │ │ │ └── route.ts 319 │ │ ├── agent/ 320 │ │ │ └── suggestions/route.ts 321 │ │ └── ai/ # AI相关路由 (Vercel AI SDK) 322 │ │ ├── chat/route.ts 323 │ │ └── completion/route.ts 324 │ ├── components # UI组件 (daisyUI sm) 325 │ │ ├── ui/ # 基础UI组件 326 │ │ └── layout/ # 布局组件 327 │ ├── lib # 核心库 328 │ │ ├── api/ # API客户端 329 │ │ ├── hooks/ # React Query hooks 330 │ │ ├── query/ # Query配置 331 │ │ └── ai/ # Vercel AI SDK配置 332 │ ├── services # Service Layer (业务逻辑) 333 │ │ ├── taskService.ts 334 │ │ ├── summaryService.ts 335 │ │ ├── agentService.ts 336 │ │ └── aiService.ts # Vercel AI SDK集成 337 │ ├── repositories # Repository Layer (数据访问) 338 │ │ ├── taskRepository.ts 339 │ │ ├── userRepository.ts 340 │ │ └── base/ 341 │ ├── types # TypeScript类型定义 342 │ │ ├── api.ts 343 │ │ ├── task.ts 344 │ │ ├── summary.ts 345 │ │ └── ai.ts # AI相关类型 346 │ ├── styles # 样式文件 (紧凑主题) 347 │ └── utils # 工具函数 348 ├── tailwind.config.js # Tailwind配置 (紧凑spacing) 349 ├── next-i18next.config.js 350 ├── package.json 351 └── README.md 352 ``` 353 354 ## 8. AI 集成与第三方框架文档获取 355 356 ### Vercel AI SDK 集成规范 357 358 所有 AI 相关的路由都必须使用 Vercel AI SDK，包括： 359 360 ```typescript 361 // src/app/api/ai/chat/route.ts (使用 Vercel AI SDK) 362 import { openai } from '@ai-sdk/openai' 363 import { streamText } from 'ai' 364 365 export async function POST(req: Request) { 366 const { messages } = await req.json() 367 368 const result = await streamText({ 369 model: openai('gpt-4'), 370 messages 371 }) 372 373 return result.toAIStreamResponse() 374 } 375 376 // src/app/api/ai/completion/route.ts 377 import { openai } from '@ai-sdk/openai' 378 import { generateText } from 'ai' 379 380 export async function POST(req: Request) { 381 const { prompt, options } = await req.json() 382 383 const { text } = await generateText({ 384 model: openai('gpt-4'), 385 prompt, 386 ...options 387 }) 388 389 return Response.json({ text }) 390 } 391 ``` 392 393 ### AI 服务层实现示例 394 395 ```typescript 396 // src/services/aiService.ts 397 import { openai } from '@ai-sdk/openai' 398 import { generateText, streamText } from 'ai' 399 400 export class AIService { 401 async generateSummary(tasks: Task[], type: SummaryType): Promise { 402 const prompt = this.buildSummaryPrompt(tasks, type) 403 404 const { text } = await generateText({ 405 model: openai('gpt-4'), 406 prompt, 407 temperature: 0.7 408 }) 409 410 return text 411 } 412 413 async streamChat(messages: Message[]): Promise { 414 const result = await streamText({ 415 model: openai('gpt-4'), 416 messages 417 }) 418 419 return result.textStream 420 } 421 422 private buildSummaryPrompt(tasks: Task[], type: SummaryType): string { 423 // 根据任务数据和类型构建 prompt 424 return `根据以下任务数据生成${type}总结...` 425 } 426 } 427 ``` 428 429 --- 430 431 ## 9. 设计原则总结 432 433 ### UI/UX 设计原则 434 435 - **紧凑布局**：所有组件使用 daisyUI 的 sm size，减少不必要的空白 436 - **一致性**：统一的间距、字体大小和组件尺寸 437 - **响应式**：在紧凑的基础上保持良好的移动端体验 438 439 ### 架构设计原则 440 441 - **分层清晰**：Controller → Service → Repository 三层架构 442 - **AI 友好**：标准化的接口、清晰的命名、完整的类型定义 443 - **可维护性**：单一职责、依赖注入、错误处理统一 444 - **性能优化**：React Query 缓存、数据库查询优化 445 446 ### 开发规范 447 448 - **TypeScript 严格模式**：确保类型安全 449 - **统一的错误处理**：标准化的错误响应格式 450 - **API 文档化**：清晰的接口定义和注释 451 - **测试覆盖**：单元测试和集成测试 452 - **第三方框架使用**：开发时需参考最新的官方文档，确保使用最佳实践 453 - Vercel AI SDK: 流式响应和文本生成的标准实现 454 - React Query: 数据获取、缓存和状态同步 455 - daisyUI: 组件 size 属性统一使用 sm 456 - Prisma: 数据库操作和类型安全 457 458 --- 459 460 以上优化方案在原有功能基础上，强化了紧凑布局设计、React Query 状态管理、后端分层架构等关键特性，使项目更加 AI 友好且具备良好的扩展性与可维护性。 461 Total lines in file: 461 ","is_error":false,"request_id":"","tool_use_id":"toolu_012d9JFtbgsLzS87Say5Yn9Z"}},{"id":2,"type":4,"ide_state_node":{"workspaceFolders":[{"repositoryRoot":"/Users/luyao/learn-front-end/work-mind","folderRoot":"/Users/luyao/learn-front-end/work-mind"}],"workspaceFoldersUnchanged":false}}],"timestamp":"2025-07-03T15:29:24.277Z","response_text":"Now I understand the project requirements. \ud83d\udcca Overview

\n Website Redesign: 60% complete (on track)

\n \n

\ud83d\udd0d Insights & Patterns

\n

\n AI analysis reveals that you're most efficient when working on documentation and \n writing tasks in the morning, while coding and technical tasks perform better \n in the afternoon. Your break patterns suggest optimal 15-minute breaks every 90 minutes.\n

\n

I've successfully created comprehensive HTML prototypes for the Work Mind application based on the project requirements. Here's a summary of what I've delivered: ## **Prototypes Created** ### 1. **Index Page** (`index.html`) - Overview of all prototypes with navigation - Clean landing page showcasing key features - Responsive grid layout for prototype cards ### 2. **Dashboard** (`dashboard-prototype.html`) - Main overview with stats cards and recent tasks - Notion-inspired sidebar navigation - Quick actions and AI insights panel - Responsive design with mobile considerations ### 3. **Task Management** (`tasks-prototype.html`) - Complete task list with filtering and search - CRUD operations with modal for new task creation - Status management and project organization - Compact table layout with hover effects ### 4. **Task Detail** (`task-detail-prototype.html`) - Individual task editing with inline editing - Time tracking functionality with timer - Tag management and project association - Auto-save functionality simulation ### 5. **AI Summary Reports** (`summary-prototype.html`) - Daily/Weekly/Monthly report tabs - AI-generated content with insights - Data visualization placeholders - Export functionality and recommendations panel ### 6. **AI Agent** (`agent-prototype.html`) - Chat interface with AI assistant - Real-time suggestions and priority recommendations - Quick action buttons and typing indicators - Interactive conversation flow ### 7. **Settings** (`settings-prototype.html`) - Comprehensive user preferences - Internationalization controls (EN/ZH) - AI configuration with sliders - Profile management and privacy settings ## **Design Features Implemented** ### **Notion-Inspired Design** - ✅ Clean typography with system fonts - ✅ Subtle shadows and borders - ✅ Modern color palette (whites, grays, blue accents) - ✅ Card-based layouts - ✅ Compact spacing (daisyUI sm size equivalent) ### **Interactive Elements** - ✅ Hover effects and transitions - ✅ Modal dialogs for task creation - ✅ Toggle switches and form controls - ✅ Real-time chat simulation - ✅ Timer functionality - ✅ Auto-save indicators ### **Responsive Design** - ✅ Mobile-first approach - ✅ Collapsible sidebar on mobile - ✅ Flexible grid layouts - ✅ Touch-friendly interface elements ### **Key Functionality Demonstrated** - ✅ Task CRUD operations - ✅ Time tracking with timer - ✅ AI chat interface - ✅ Report generation simulation - ✅ Multi-language support - ✅ Settings management - ✅ Data visualization placeholders ## **How to Use** 1. **Open the prototypes**: Start with `prototypes/index.html` in your browser 2. **Navigate between pages**: Use the sidebar navigation or index page links 3. **Test interactions**: Click buttons, fill forms, and interact with elements 4. **View on mobile**: Resize browser window to see responsive behavior ## **Design Decisions** - **Inline CSS**: Used for prototype simplicity and self-contained files - **Semantic HTML**: Proper structure for accessibility - **Progressive Enhancement**: Core functionality works without JavaScript - **Performance**: Lightweight with minimal dependencies - **Consistency**: Unified design system across all prototypes These prototypes provide a comprehensive visualization of the Work Mind application interface, allowing stakeholders to experience the user flow and design aesthetic before development begins. The clean, Notion-inspired design emphasizes usability while maintaining the compact layout requirements specified in the project documentation. ✅ Clean typography with system fonts - ✅ Subtle shadows and borders - ✅ Modern color palette (whites, grays, blue accents) - ✅ Card-based layouts - ✅ Compact spacing (daisyUI sm size equivalent) ### **Interactive Elements** - ✅ Hover effects and transitions - ✅ Modal dialogs for task creation - ✅ Toggle switches and form controls - ✅ Real-time chat simulation - ✅ Timer functionality - ✅ Auto-save indicators ### **Responsive Design** - ✅ Mobile-first approach - ✅ Collapsible sidebar on mobile - ✅ Flexible grid layouts - ✅ Touch-friendly interface elements ### **Key Functionality Demonstrated** - ✅ Task CRUD operations - ✅ Time tracking with timer - ✅ AI chat interface - ✅ Report generation simulation - ✅ Multi-language support - ✅ Settings management - ✅ Data visualization placeholders ## **How to Use** 1. **Open the prototypes**: Start with `prototypes/index.html` in your browser 2. **Navigate between pages**: Use the sidebar navigation or index page links 3. **Test interactions**: Click buttons, fill forms, and interact with elements 4. **View on mobile**: Resize browser window to see responsive behavior ## **Design Decisions** - **Inline CSS**: Used for prototype simplicity and self-contained files - **Semantic HTML**: Proper structure for accessibility - **Progressive Enhancement**: Core functionality works without JavaScript - **Performance**: Lightweight with minimal dependencies - **Consistency**: Unified design system across all prototypes These prototypes provide a comprehensive visualization of the Work Mind application interface, allowing stakeholders to experience the user flow and design aesthetic before development begins. The clean, Notion-inspired design emphasizes usability while maintaining the compact layout requirements specified in the project documentation. **前端**：Next.js (app/目录)、React + daisyUI 10 - **样式**：Tailwind CSS + daisyUI 组件库（所有 size 相关属性统一使用 sm） 11 - **状态管理**：React Query (TanStack Query) - 用于数据获取、缓存和状态管理 12 - **国际化**：next-i18next 或 next-intl 13 - **后端**：Next.js API Routes（分层架构设计，AI 友好） 14 - **数据库**：PostgreSQL (Prisma ORM) 15 - **AI 服务**：Vercel AI SDK + OpenAI API / 其他 LLM 16 - **可视化**：Recharts / Chart.js 17 18 --- 19 20 ## 3. 功能架构设计 21 22 ```mermaid 23 flowchart LR 24 subgraph Frontend["前端架构 (紧凑布局)"] 25 UI[UI Components
daisyUI sm size] 26 RQ[React Query
状态管理 & 缓存] 27 API_CLIENT[API Client
统一接口层] 28 end 29 subgraph Backend["后端分层架构 (AI友好)"] 30 CONTROLLER[Controller Layer
路由处理] 31 SERVICE[Service Layer
业务逻辑] 32 REPOSITORY[Repository Layer
数据访问] 33 DB[(PostgreSQL)] 34 AI[OpenAI API] 35 end 36 UI --> RQ 37 RQ --> API_CLIENT 38 API_CLIENT --> CONTROLLER 39 CONTROLLER --> SERVICE 40 SERVICE --> REPOSITORY 41 SERVICE --> AI 42 REPOSITORY --> DB 43 ``` 44 45 ### 前端架构特点 46 47 - **紧凑布局设计**：所有组件间距、padding、margin 采用紧凑设计 48 - **React Query 状态管理**：统一数据获取、缓存、同步和错误处理 49 - **AI 友好接口层**：标准化的 API 调用接口，便于 AI 理解和维护 50 51 ### 后端分层架构 52 53 - **Controller Layer**：处理 HTTP 请求，参数验证，响应格式化 54 - **Service Layer**：核心业务逻辑，AI 集成，数据处理 55 - **Repository Layer**：数据库操作抽象，查询优化 56 57 ### 核心功能模块 58 59 - **工作记录模块**：CRUD 任务记录；状态更新；标签 & 项目关联 60 - **智能总结模块**：手动触发，支持定期触发；调用 AI 生成报告；展示图表 61 - **Agent 助手模块**：实时分析；预测 & 建议；优先级排序 62 63 --- 64 65 ## 4. 数据库模型设计 66 67 ```prisma 68 model User { 69 id String @id @default(cuid()) 70 name String 71 email String @unique 72 tasks Task[] 73 } 74 75 model Project { 76 id String @id @default(cuid()) 77 name String 78 tasks Task[] 79 } 80 81 model Tag { 82 id String @id @default(cuid()) 83 name String @unique 84 tasks Task[] @relation(references: [id]) 85 } 86 87 model Task { 88 id String @id @default(cuid()) 89 user User @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id]) 90 userId String 91 project Project? @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id]) 92 projectId String? 93 title String 94 description String? 95 status String @default("pending") // pending, in_progress, completed, canceled 96 startTime DateTime 97 endTime DateTime? 98 duration Int? // minutes 99 tags Tag[] @relation(references: [id]) 100 createdAt DateTime @default(now()) 101 updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt 102 } 103 ``` 104 105 --- 106 107 ## 5. 主要页面与组件结构 108 109 ```text 110 /src/app # Next.js App Router 111 |_ layout.tsx # 根布局 (紧凑设计) 112 |_ page.tsx # 仪表盘 113 |_ tasks 114 |_ page.tsx # 任务列表 115 |_ [id] 116 |_ page.tsx # 任务详情与编辑 117 |_ summary 118 |_ daily 119 |_ page.tsx # 日报 120 |_ weekly 121 |_ page.tsx # 周报 122 |_ monthly 123 |_ page.tsx # 月报 124 |_ settings 125 |_ page.tsx # 个人设置 / 国际化切换 126 127 /src/components # UI组件 (daisyUI sm size) 128 |_ ui/ 129 |_ TaskList.tsx # 任务列表 (紧凑布局) 130 |_ TaskForm.tsx # 新建/编辑任务 (sm size) 131 |_ SummaryCard.tsx # 总结报告卡片 (紧凑) 132 |_ ChartPanel.tsx # 可视化图表容器 133 |_ Sidebar.tsx # 侧边导航 (紧凑) 134 |_ i18nSwitcher.tsx # 语言切换 135 136 /src/lib # React Query & API层 137 |_ api/ 138 |_ client.ts # API客户端配置 139 |_ tasks.ts # 任务相关API 140 |_ summary.ts # 总结相关API 141 |_ agent.ts # AI助手API 142 |_ hooks/ 143 |_ useTasks.ts # 任务数据hooks 144 |_ useSummary.ts # 总结数据hooks 145 |_ useAgent.ts # AI助手hooks 146 |_ query/ 147 |_ queryClient.ts # React Query配置 148 |_ keys.ts # Query keys管理 149 ``` 150 151 --- 152 153 ## 6. API 接口设计 (分层架构) 154 155 ### Controller Layer - API Routes 156 157 | 路由 | 方法 | 描述 | 请求体 / 参数 | 返回值 | 158 | ---------------------- | ------ | -------------------- | --------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | 159 | /api/tasks | GET | 获取当前用户所有任务 | page, pageSize, status, project, tag | Task\[] | 160 | /api/tasks | POST | 新建任务 | { title, desc, startTime, projectId, tagIds } | Task | 161 | /api/tasks/\[id] | GET | 获取单个任务详情 | id | Task | 162 | /api/tasks/\[id] | PUT | 更新任务 | { status, endTime, duration, ... } | Task | 163 | /api/tasks/\[id] | DELETE | 删除任务 | id | { success: boolean } | 164 | /api/summary | POST | 生成 AI 总结报告 | { type: daily/weekly/monthly, date } | { report: string, stats } | 165 | /api/agent/suggestions | GET | 获取智能建议 | ?date, ?projectId | Suggestion\[] | 166 | /api/ai/chat | POST | AI 对话接口 | { messages: Message[], stream?: boolean } | StreamingResponse | 167 | /api/ai/completion | POST | AI 文本补全 | { prompt: string, options?: CompletionOpts } | CompletionResponse | 168 169 ### Service Layer - 业务逻辑 170 171 ```typescript 172 // src/services/taskService.ts 173 export class TaskService { 174 constructor(private taskRepository: TaskRepository) {} 175 176 async getTasks(userId: string, filters: TaskFilters): Promise 177 async createTask(userId: string, taskData: CreateTaskDto): Promise 178 async updateTask(taskId: string, updateData: UpdateTaskDto): Promise 179 async deleteTask(taskId: string): Promise 180 } 181 182 // src/services/summaryService.ts 183 export class SummaryService { 184 constructor(private taskRepository: TaskRepository, private aiService: AIService) {} 185 186 async generateSummary(type: SummaryType, date: Date): Promise 187 } 188 189 // src/services/agentService.ts 190 export class AgentService { 191 constructor(private taskRepository: TaskRepository, private aiService: AIService) {} 192 193 async getSuggestions(userId: string, context?: AgentContext): Promise 194 } 195 196 // src/services/aiService.ts (使用 Vercel AI SDK) 197 export class AIService { 198 constructor(private openai: OpenAI) {} 199 200 async generateCompletion(prompt: string, options?: CompletionOptions): Promise 201 async streamChat(messages: Message[]): Promise 202 async generateSummary(tasks: Task[], type: SummaryType): Promise 203 async generateSuggestions(context: AgentContext): Promise 204 } 205 ``` 206 207 ### Repository Layer - 数据访问 208 209 ```typescript 210 // src/repositories/taskRepository.ts 211 export class TaskRepository { 212 constructor(private prisma: PrismaClient) {} 213 214 async findMany(filters: TaskFilters): Promise 215 async findById(id: string): Promise 216 async create(data: CreateTaskData): Promise 217 async update(id: string, data: UpdateTaskData): Promise 218 async delete(id: string): Promise 219 } 220 ``` 221 222 --- 223 224 ## 7. 实现步骤与文件结构 225 226 1. **初始化项目** 227 228 - `npx create-next-app@latest --typescript --app` 229 - 安装依赖：`daisyui`, `tailwindcss`, `prisma`, `@prisma/client`, `@tanstack/react-query`, `ai`, `@ai-sdk/openai`, `next-i18next` 等 230 231 2. **配置 Tailwind & daisyUI (紧凑布局)** 232 233 - 更新 `tailwind.config.js` 234 - 配置 daisyUI 主题，设置默认 size 为 sm 235 - 自定义紧凑布局的 spacing 配置 236 237 3. **React Query 状态管理配置** 238 239 - 配置 QueryClient 和 QueryClientProvider 240 - 设置缓存策略和错误处理 241 - 创建自定义 hooks 封装 API 调用 242 243 4. **国际化集成** 244 245 - 配置 `next-i18next.config.js` 246 - 创建 `public/locales/{en,zh}/common.json` 247 248 5. **数据库 & ORM** 249 250 - 编写 `schema.prisma` 251 - 运行 `npx prisma migrate dev` 252 253 6. **后端分层架构搭建** 254 255 - 创建 Repository Layer (数据访问层) 256 - 创建 Service Layer (业务逻辑层) 257 - 创建 Controller Layer (API 路由层) 258 - 实现依赖注入和错误处理 259 260 7. **前端组件开发 (紧凑设计)** 261 262 - 搭建基本布局与导航 (使用 daisyUI sm size) 263 - 实现任务列表与 CRUD (紧凑布局) 264 - 集成 React Query 进行数据管理 265 266 8. **AI 接口集成 (使用 Vercel AI SDK)** 267 268 - 在 Service Layer 中集成 Vercel AI SDK 269 - 实现流式响应和文本补全功能 270 - 配置 AI 路由使用 Vercel AI SDK 的标准模式 271 - 实现 prompt 模板 & 并发调用控制 272 - 通过 Repository 层访问历史数据 273 274 9. **可视化与报告** 275 276 - 使用 Recharts 绘制时间趋势图、饼图等 277 - 实现紧凑的图表布局 278 279 10. **Agent 模块** 280 281 - 开发 AgentService 业务逻辑 282 - 利用历史数据 & AI 生成预测与建议 283 - 通过 React Query 管理 AI 响应缓存 284 285 11. **测试 & 部署** 286 287 - 单元测试 (Jest) 288 - API 层测试 289 - 部署到 Vercel / Netlify 290 291 **项目文件夹结构 (AI 友好分层架构)**： 292 293 ``` 294 ├── public 295 │ └── locales 296 │ ├── en 297 │ └── zh 298 ├── prisma 299 │ └── schema.prisma 300 ├── src 301 │ ├── app # Next.js App Router 302 │ │ ├── layout.tsx # 根布局 (紧凑设计) 303 │ │ ├── page.tsx # 仪表盘 304 │ │ ├── tasks/ 305 │ │ │ ├── page.tsx 306 │ │ │ └── [id]/page.tsx 307 │ │ ├── summary/ 308 │ │ │ ├── daily/page.tsx 309 │ │ │ ├── weekly/page.tsx 310 │ │ │ └── monthly/page.tsx 311 │ │ ├── settings/ 312 │ │ │ └── page.tsx 313 │ │ └── api # API Routes (App Router) 314 │ │ ├── tasks/ 315 │ │ │ ├── route.ts 316 │ │ │ └── [id]/route.ts 317 │ │ ├── summary/ 318 │ │ │ └── route.ts 319 │ │ ├── agent/ 320 │ │ │ └── suggestions/route.ts 321 │ │ └── ai/ # AI相关路由 (Vercel AI SDK) 322 │ │ ├── chat/route.ts 323 │ │ └── completion/route.ts 324 │ ├── components # UI组件 (daisyUI sm) 325 │ │ ├── ui/ # 基础UI组件 326 │ │ └── layout/ # 布局组件 327 │ ├── lib # 核心库 328 │ │ ├── api/ # API客户端 329 │ │ ├── hooks/ # React Query hooks 330 │ │ ├── query/ # Query配置 331 │ │ └── ai/ # Vercel AI SDK配置 332 │ ├── services # Service Layer (业务逻辑) 333 │ │ ├── taskService.ts 334 │ │ ├── summaryService.ts 335 │ │ ├── agentService.ts 336 │ │ └── aiService.ts # Vercel AI SDK集成 337 │ ├── repositories # Repository Layer (数据访问) 338 │ │ ├── taskRepository.ts 339 │ │ ├── userRepository.ts 340 │ │ └── base/ 341 │ ├── types # TypeScript类型定义 342 │ │ ├── api.ts 343 │ │ ├── task.ts 344 │ │ ├── summary.ts 345 │ │ └── ai.ts # AI相关类型 346 │ ├── styles # 样式文件 (紧凑主题) 347 │ └── utils # 工具函数 348 ├── tailwind.config.js # Tailwind配置 (紧凑spacing) 349 ├── next-i18next.config.js 350 ├── package.json 351 └── README.md 352 ``` 353 354 ## 8. AI 集成与第三方框架文档获取 355 356 ### Vercel AI SDK 集成规范 357 358 所有 AI 相关的路由都必须使用 Vercel AI SDK，包括： 359 360 ```typescript 361 // src/app/api/ai/chat/route.ts (使用 Vercel AI SDK) 362 import { openai } from '@ai-sdk/openai' 363 import { streamText } from 'ai' 364 365 export async function POST(req: Request) { 366 const { messages } = await req.json() 367 368 const result = await streamText({ 369 model: openai('gpt-4'), 370 messages 371 }) 372 373 return result.toAIStreamResponse() 374 } 375 376 // src/app/api/ai/completion/route.ts 377 import { openai } from '@ai-sdk/openai' 378 import { generateText } from 'ai' 379 380 export async function POST(req: Request) { 381 const { prompt, options } = await req.json() 382 383 const { text } = await generateText({ 384 model: openai('gpt-4'), 385 prompt, 386 ...options 387 }) 388 389 return Response.json({ text }) 390 } 391 ``` 392 393 ### AI 服务层实现示例 394 395 ```typescript 396 // src/services/aiService.ts 397 import { openai } from '@ai-sdk/openai' 398 import { generateText, streamText } from 'ai' 399 400 export class AIService { 401 async generateSummary(tasks: Task[], type: SummaryType): Promise { 402 const prompt = this.buildSummaryPrompt(tasks, type) 403 404 const { text } = await generateText({ 405 model: openai('gpt-4'), 406 prompt, 407 temperature: 0.7 408 }) 409 410 return text 411 } 412 413 async streamChat(messages: Message[]): Promise { 414 const result = await streamText({ 415 model: openai('gpt-4'), 416 messages 417 }) 418 419 return result.textStream 420 } 421 422 private buildSummaryPrompt(tasks: Task[], type: SummaryType): string { 423 // 根据任务数据和类型构建 prompt 424 return `根据以下任务数据生成${type}总结...` 425 } 426 } 427 ``` 428 429 --- 430 431 ## 9. 设计原则总结 432 433 ### UI/UX 设计原则 434 435 - **紧凑布局**：所有组件使用 daisyUI 的 sm size，减少不必要的空白 436 - **一致性**：统一的间距、字体大小和组件尺寸 437 - **响应式**：在紧凑的基础上保持良好的移动端体验 438 439 ### 架构设计原则 440 441 - **分层清晰**：Controller → Service → Repository 三层架构 442 - **AI 友好**：标准化的接口、清晰的命名、完整的类型定义 443 - **可维护性**：单一职责、依赖注入、错误处理统一 444 - **性能优化**：React Query 缓存、数据库查询优化 445 446 ### 开发规范 447 448 - **TypeScript 严格模式**：确保类型安全 449 - **统一的错误处理**：标准化的错误响应格式 450 - **API 文档化**：清晰的接口定义和注释 451 - **测试覆盖**：单元测试和集成测试 452 - **第三方框架使用**：开发时需参考最新的官方文档，确保使用最佳实践 453 - Vercel AI SDK: 流式响应和文本生成的标准实现 454 - React Query: 数据获取、缓存和状态同步 455 - daisyUI: 组件 size 属性统一使用 sm 456 - Prisma: 数据库操作和类型安全 457 458 --- 459 460 以上优化方案在原有功能基础上，强化了紧凑布局设计、React Query 状态管理、后端分层架构等关键特性，使项目更加 AI 友好且具备良好的扩展性与可维护性。 461 Total lines in file: 461 ","isError":false,"requestId":""}},"988a81f9-dcd1-42c6-896d-e9d6fb93597c;toolu_015cryTRm7oJSVL6sg4ZJsGA":{"requestId":"988a81f9-dcd1-42c6-896d-e9d6fb93597c","toolUseId":"toolu_015cryTRm7oJSVL6sg4ZJsGA","phase":5,"result":{"text":"Here are the results from executing the command. 0 ","isError":false,"requestId":""}},"6d70333b-b334-47c7-83d8-9cb04c48e41b;toolu_0141Zg4L1RWJJ96vbcCTHW6W":{"requestId":"6d70333b-b334-47c7-83d8-9cb04c48e41b","toolUseId":"toolu_0141Zg4L1RWJJ96vbcCTHW6W","phase":5,"result":{"text":"File saved. Generate the following files: 1. **generate-biz-component.md**: Business component generation rules - **Output files**: - `index.ts` (component exports) - `interface.ts` (TypeScript interfaces and types) - `[ComponentName].stories.tsx` (Storybook stories) - `[ComponentName].tsx` (main React component) - **Requirements**: Follow modern React patterns, include proper TypeScript typing, and Storybook integration 2. **generate-model-repository.md**: Data layer generation rules following Trevor Lasn's Three Layers pattern - **Step 0**: Analyze existing Prisma schema before code generation - **Output**: Prisma schema definitions and repository layer code - **Requirements**: Handle schema modifications cautiously, provide migration guidance, follow repository pattern 3. **generate-api-service.md**: API and service layer generation rules - **Output**: Next.js API routes and service layer code - **Requirements**: Include proper error handling, validation, and follow RESTful conventions 4. **generate-page-component.md**: Page component generation with data integration - **Architecture**: Follow Three-Layer Data Architecture pattern - Layer 1: Server Components (page.tsx) that integrate React Query hooks - Layer 2: React Query hooks that encapsulate data logic - Layer 3: UI components for user interactions - **Requirements**: - Use React Query for client-side rendering - Include proper query keys hierarchy - Implement optimistic updates with rollback mechanisms - Configure appropriate staleTime/gcTime for performance - Integrate with API services and business components Each rule file should be comprehensive, include specific examples, and follow consistent naming conventions and project structure patterns.","rich_text_json_repr":{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Create four LangGPT-style code generation rule files in the `@instructions` directory.
